title: Road to AGI: A Road with Curves
description: Progress in Artificial Intelligence is often perceived as an exponential curve that only accelerates into an unpredictable chaos of daily headlines. The reality is more complex—and more fascinating. AI advances in a series of overlapping waves, where each new capability emerges, accelerates, and eventually hits a ceiling. Where are we really on this winding path? Why do today’s models seem to stall at certain tasks while excelling at others? And most importantly, what breakthroughs still lie ahead on the road to true AGI?Discover exactly where we stand on the curve today, which ceilings we are currently hitting, and which critical pieces are still missing to unlock the next major stage of artificial intelligence.

title: The future of Software Development in the age of AI
description: In a time of rapid change where AI is a board-level conversation on it’s impact on software development. How do developers respond? We will explore why the future is about giving developers smarter tools to build smarter apps on smarter platforms. Discover the fundamental shifts that will empower developers to: focus on simplicity, experiment quickly, build agentic solutions, shift down responsibilities through platform engineering, and use AI assistance. Join Abdel Sghiouar, for a look into the future of our industry.

title: Where the AI You Don’t Know Yet Is Born
description: 1. Víctor Rotellar – Coordinador – Xarxa RDI-IA: Catalonian AI Innovation Hub2. Víctor Cepero – CEO – Brainmapp3. Ion Padilla – Cyclezen194. Ricard Baulíes i Fusté – CEO – Noctua5. Lucas Alejo Dening -CEO – ReadiseasyWhile the market talks about products and applications, the next generation of deep-tech AI is being developed in Catalan laboratories. We will explore how the country’s research system generates these technologies and what mechanisms allow them to be transformed into real-world impact.

title: Career Pivot to AI Experience Engineering: The 2026 Tech Market
description: 3 speakers:1. Kristina Talova – COO Education director at SheAI 2. Maja Zavrsnik – CMO at SheAI 3. Stephany Oliveros – CEO & Co-founder at SheAIThe 2026 tech market is exploding with AI roles beyond traditional engineering. Discover AI Experience Engineering – applying AI to workflows, products, education, and operations. SheAI explores why diverse talent isn’t optional and the unique value women bring to AI deployment. Learn which roles are in demand, essential skills from AI literacy to ethical design, and a concrete roadmap to pivot into this AI-first landscape.

title: Experis Intro
description: 1. Fernando Aguilar – Head of Experis Academy at EXPERIS.2. Sandra Gómez Palomares – Professional Recruitment Leader IT at EXPERIS.

title: Transforming S2P through AI and Emerging Technologies
description: 1. Jurgen Martens – Capability Lead – Source to Pay at Bayer2. Jose Carlos Esclapes – Capability Lead – Source to Pay at Bayer3. Lorena Gonzalez Juste – Digital Transformation Lead at BayerAt Bayer, scaling AI successfully is less about algorithms and more about how people, processes, and ownership models come together. This session presents a concrete case where AI and emerging technologies delivers measurable business value through close collaboration between Business teams in S2P and Finance and IT, guided by our way of working which is Dynamic Shared Ownership (DSO).

title: Efficient Intelligence: Agentic Systems Without Heavy GPUs or Custom Training
description: 1. Pablo Ramos (moderator) – Director of Partnerships and Ecosystem, Huawei Cloud Iberia at Huawei2. Aaron Ramson – Chief AI Officer (CAIO) at Thinkia3. Oriol Jofra – CEO at Digital MakerEfficient AI: Building Agentic Systems Without GPUs or Training.” A practical approach to leveraging Small Language Models (SLMs) and In-Context Learning to create autonomous agents without massive hardware costs. It demonstrates that effective orchestration and system architecture are often more critical than raw model size.

title: Decoding Luxury: The LVMH Data Challenge x Albert School
description: 1. Anna Lopez Estela – Albert School – General Manager2. Yagiz Yigit – Albert School – Lecturer & Ex-Moody’s, AIG I PhD Candidate3. Elena Garrigues – Albert School & Garrigues – Professor & Global Managing Director and International Business Development Executive specializing in business transformation, innovation, and corporate ethics4. Guillermo Fernández García – CoFounder, COO & CFO @Relash | Professor of Accounting, Finance and Operations @AlbertSchool | PhD Candidate | ExDeloitte & ExAccenture Manager | Entrepreneur, Psychologist and CommunicatorTeam up to tackle real business challenges using data from Louis Vuitton and the LVMH Group. Dive into exclusive datasets, uncover insights, and make bold strategic decisions that mirror those shaping the future of global luxury. Immersive, competitive, and fast‑paced, this challenge is built for those ready to prove their talent in strategy, data, and innovation.

title: Getting Started with Google Antigravity
description: In this codelab, you will learn about Google Antigravity (referred to as Antigravity for the rest of the document), an agentic development platform, evolving the IDE into the agent-first era. Unlike standard coding assistants that just autocomplete lines, Antigravity provides a “Mission Control” for managing autonomous agents that can plan, code, and even browse the web to help you build. Antigravity is designed as an agent-first platform. It presupposes that the AI is not just a tool for writing code but an autonomous actor capable of planning, executing, validating, and iterating on complex engineering tasks with minimal human intervention.

title: Demystifying Intelligence in AI Agents
description: 1. Steven Latré – AI Chief Officer at Openchip2. Bohdan Denysenko – Senior AI Engineer – Multi-Agent LLM Systems at Openchip3. Igor Peric – Director AI Applications at OpenchipConnect strategy and engineering to explain how modern agentic systems are built. After a high‑level view of deploying secure, sovereign AI, we dive into the technical foundations: transformers, attention, model internals, and the math behind large language models. Participants walk through the full GPT‑2 architecture and build it from scratch in Python, gaining a fundamental understanding of how agents work and how to build them responsibly.

title: UNO Q Robot Dog: Dual-Brain Control in Action
description: Discover advanced robotics with Arduino UNO Q through a four-legged robot dog remotely controlled via dual-band Wi-Fi. UNO Q’s dual-brain architecture combines MCU real-time motor control with MPU high-level processing and sensor fusion, enabling smooth movement and responsive interaction in a compact, connected platform.

title: When 3rd-Parties Invade Your Domain: Protect Your Business from External Chaos
description: In this talk, we will explore the rapid journey that has brought us here: from early models like GPT-2 and GPT-3, to the global impact of ChatGPT and the current era of multimodal models. We will see how, in just a few years, artificial intelligence has evolved from merely understanding and generating text to reasoning, seeing, hearing, and acting—paving the way for a new paradigm of more autonomous and adaptive systems.

title: The Orbital Reckoning: Why the next balance of Power will be decided in Space
description: The world is entering an orbital era where deterrence, infrastructure, and national resilience are shaped by what happens above Earth. As a European founder building in this domain, I’ll reveal the real forces unfolding in orbit – silent interference, environmental volatility, and new power dynamics – and share the strategic shifts Europe must make now to stay competitive in a rapidly weaponised space environment.

title: Success.exe: The discipline behind performance
description: In an era of instant gratification, peak performance is often misunderstood as talent—or luck. In reality, sustained success is rarely accidental; it is the result of deliberate structure and disciplined execution. In this opening session at Talent Arena, we will break down the core variables behind high performance: Talent, Willingness, and Time. From the “garage mindset” that shaped HP’s early culture to the relentless precision of athletes and esports legends like Stephen Curry or Daigo Umehara, we will explore what separates potential from mastery. Through real examples, we will challenge the myth of overnight success and examine why discipline—not inspiration—is the true operating system behind long-term achievement. Attendees will leave with a clearer framework to rethink performance in a tech-driven world where execution is the ultimate competitive advantage.

title: R&D in the era of AI
description: AI is causing a technological revolution in multiple fields. In this talk, we will see how it is changing R&D, showing AI applied to real industrial problems and introducing different ways of using it beyond chatbots. The talk will be pedagogic in nature, assuming no previous knowledge of AI, and will also present potential dangers and limitations of current AI models.

title: In the spotlight: Know your potential adversaries and strengthen your defenses
description: The session shows how to identify and profile adversaries by analyzing tactics and patterns, turn intelligence into Adversary Emulation scenarios, and run realistic simulations. It allows you to measure detections, uncover gaps, validate resilience, and assess defenses against advanced attacks—including EDR evasions—with a threat-informed approach.

title: When AI Joins the Team: New Rules of the Game
description: 1. Víctor Agramunt (Moderator) – Data & AI Manager at Seidor2. Xavier Roig Gràcia – Chief Information Officer at Grupo Familia Torres3. Iñaki Suárez – Chief Technology Officer at Basque AI Center (BAIC)4. Fernando Marín Gurrea – Head of the AI Office at Barcelona Innovació i Tecnologia – Ajuntament de Barcelona5. Xavier Manzanera – Head of Digital Office, Data & AI at EsteveAI is changing the rules of the game in technology projects and teams, but not everyone is integrating it in the same way or achieving the same results. What makes the difference? This session isn’t about theories—we want to share real experiences from different organizations to help us understand: what’s working and what’s not, what changes need to be addressed, how leadership evolves when AI becomes part of the team’s daily work, and how decision-making shifts. Don’t miss it.

title: Ask me anything about Java
description: 1. Nacho Cougil – Principal Software Engineer at Dynatrace2. Anyul Rivas Oropeza – Senior Architect at GFT

title: IndraMind: Sovereign AI Capabilities, Cyber-Resilience, and Autonomous Operations
description: 1. Roberto Espina Machon – Global Director of Cybersecurity at Indra Group2. Marco Antonio Sotelo Monge – Expert, Cyber Defense Strategy Department at Indra Group3. Javier Corchero Tellez – AI Operations Expert at Indra Group4. Xavier Cabre Bozal – Director of Cyber Defense Programs at Indra GroupIndraMind is the decisive step toward technological sovereignty and strategic superiority in a hybrid warfare environment. Faced with threats such as drones and cyberattacks, it integrates cognitive superiority, autonomous operations, and maximum cyber resilience; to anticipate risks, coordinate intelligent systems, and ensure operational continuity, strengthening institutional response and laying the groundwork for security and defense solutions.

title: Building the future in Rust
description: Rust is one of the most loved and wanted languages today. Its memory safety, speed, and lack of a garbage collector make it ideal for safety-critical automotive, aerospace, robotics, cloud, and embedded systems. It’s also gaining traction for production AI inference, especially high-throughput LLMs and edge deployments. Rust offers a clear path to modernize legacy systems, improve safety, and build the next generation of reliable software.

title: Open Innovation Challenge
description: 1- Quico Vidal (moderator) – Founder & CEO at Nadie2. Enric Riera – Chief Customer Officer at World2Meet3. Stefano Ceccarello – Chief Digital & Experience Officer at World2Meet4. Joan Barceló – CIO W2M5. Ana García – Dir. Marketing W2MClosing and Winner Selection – Open Innovation Challenge: Pitch Presentations of the 6 Projects

title: Radical privacy: small actions with big impact
description: The abuse of our personal data and privacy is no longer the exception, but the norm. Every day, we see headlines about security breaches exposing personal information, social networks turning our daily lives into a product, government agencies taking surveillance to the extreme, and companies profiting from every click we make. Our information is collected, stored, and exploited in ways we often don’t even realize. What’s worrying is that the trend is only heading upward, with increasingly serious impacts on our privacy and personal security. In this talk, we will highlight many of the ways and methods through which we unknowingly hand over our information, and we will explore how to apply operational security (OPSEC) principles to our everyday digital lives. We’ll discuss real changes, as well as methodologies, strategies, and small tricks that anyone can adopt to reduce their digital footprint, minimize risks, and regain some control over their online identity.

title: From Reflex to Reason: The Engineering Journey to Physical AI
description: In 2019, “Edge AI” was a feat of magic: fitting a simple keyword spotter onto a microcontroller. Today, in 2026, we are running 11-billion-parameter Vision-Language Models (VLMs) on pocket-sized silicon. But how did we get here, and where is the “intelligence of everything” actually going?

title: Technology with purpose: Making music truly inclusive
description: Technology takes on its deepest meaning when it serves people. Guided by this belief, ERNI, ABB and Fundación SIFU created an innovative project that brings music within reach of those who once thought it impossible. By combining robotics, artificial intelligence and human creativity, a young pianist with cerebral palsy, Joel Bueno, can now play a traditional piano using only his eyes, turning engineering into a powerful symbol of inclusion and emotion.

title: You Play the CTF. We Play Defense
description: 1. Cristina Pulido – Privacy Engineer Expert at Schneider Electric2, Maria Hitz – Cyber Risk & Compliance Manager at Schneider Electric3, Pol Jane – Infrastructure Security Architect Principal at Schneider Electric4, Pau Aurin – Cybersecurity Exposure Management and Deception Expert at Schneider ElectricIn this session, you’ll use your own laptop to jump into a hands‑on CTF, playing as a pentester while you explore and attack, just like a real red teamer. Then the game stops, and the blue team takes over to show you what your actions look like from the defender’s side: the alerts, the signals, and how security teams respond in real environments. It’s not just a CTF. It’s you playing the attack, and us revealing the defense in real time.

title: Life in a Tab: The Browser Ate the Desktop
description: The Web as a Platform in 2026 is a hands-on workshop designed for professional developers who want to understand, from the inside, how far the Web has truly evolved as an application platform.

title: On‑Device LLMs with Genie 2: Building a Chat App Using Qualcomm® AI Hub Apps
description: Join this hands-on workshop and learn how to run large language models fully on-device with the Qualcomm® AI Hub™ Apps. You’ll use a pretrained LLM from Qualcomm AI Hub Models to build and run a C++ ChatApp optimized to run on the Qualcomm® Hexagon™ NPU.

title: From legacy CI/CD to modern software delivery
description: A deep dive into authoring, refactoring, and troubleshooting CI/CD pipelines, supported by GitHub Copilot and GitHub Advanced Security. We’ll show how to migrate legacy pipelines powered by bash scripts into clear, reusable workflow components, implement new pipeline jobs safely, and eliminate common execution errors through precise permissions, deterministic steps, and disciplined artifact handling. A live demo will demonstrate interpreting Actions logs, isolating failing steps, and harnessing Copilot for targeted fixes. We’ll bake in supply chain security by enforcing least‑privilege workflow permissions, pinning external actions, and running security checks from GitHub Advanced Security as part of every change. Attendees will leave with repeatable patterns to accelerate delivery while raising the security and reliability bar across repositories.

title: Future-Ready: AI, Ethics & Skills That Set You apart
description: 1. Anna Lopez Estela – General Manager at Albert School2. Elena Garrigues – Professor & Global Managing Director and International Business Development Executive specializing in business transformation, innovation, and corporate ethics3. Koro Castellano – Albert School & Independent Board Member – Professor & Strategic Senior Advisor specialized in digital business transformation, content-driven platforms and technology-enabled growth, with a strong macro perspective and a pragmatic approach to innovation4. Yagiz Yigit – Albert School – Lecturer & Ex-Moody’s, AIG I PhD CandidateIn a world where automation and AI reshape work faster than education can adapt, the key differentiator is no longer access to tools but the quality of human judgment. This session explores how professionals can thrive by combining automation with ethics and human skills—rethinking education, freeing time for deep work, and staying truly human in an automated world.

title: MDM: The Silent Engine Powering AI Success
description: Martí Ganduxé – Services Installed Base Digital Senior Manager at Schneider Electric

title: Beyond Agentic AI: Designing the Future of Healthcare with Women in STEM
description: 1. Cesar Velasco (moderator) – Enterprise Innovation Director at AstraZeneca2. Cecilia Giron – Director of Data Science at Evinova3. Elena Millan – Director, RDU IT – Product Management, Knowledge Management, and AI Content Supply Chain at Alexion4. Marta Tolos – Executive Director, Enterprise AI Platforms & Services at AstraZenecaAt AstraZeneca, science, data and technology drive AI-enabled healthcare. We’re advancing from generative AI (faster thinking/learning) to agentic AI (acting across complex systems) across discovery, development, evidence and patient care. The Hub blends scientific rigor, digital expertise and responsible AI, builds the future workforce,especially women in STEM, and offers frontier opportunities, non-linear careers and purpose-led impact.

title: Back to Your Future: get ready for the tech market
description: 1. Javier Martínez – Leader – Claire Joster Technology en Claire Joster2. José María Flores – Regional Leader – Madrid en Claire Joster3. Jordi Valls – Senior Consultant 360 en Claire Joster4.Jordi Llahí – Sales and Marketing Leader en Claire JosterWe all would have benefited from knowing certain tips and lessons when we were students. Although we can’t go back, we can turn that reflection into an opportunity for others. With our knowledge, we provide students with guidance and practical tools to help them make decisions with greater confidence as they build their future. An experience that will help you travel toward the professional future you desire.

title: This is the future: programming a humanoid robot
description: In this talk I will show you how a humanoid robot works in 2026, how it is programmed, what can be done with it, what advantages artificial intelligence gives us, and where all this is heading.

title: Inside the Filter Bubble: Rethinking Recommendations in the GenAI Era
description: As generative AI becomes the primary interface to information, recommendation systems are quietly transforming into engines of interpretation. Instead of simply filtering content, GenAI systems increasingly define how knowledge is framed, explained, and contextualized—creating personalized realities at scale.This talk explores the emergence of a new kind of filter bubble, one rooted not in what is shown, but in how meaning is constructed. It examines the societal, cultural, and economic implications of AI systems that adapt narratives to individual users, potentially narrowing perspectives while appearing more helpful than ever.By reframing recommendation systems as cognitive infrastructure, the session argues for a shift in design philosophy—from maximizing engagement to preserving pluralism, serendipity, and trust. The talk concludes by outlining how industry leaders can ensure that GenAI-powered recommendations expand human understanding rather than constrain it.

title: Denshattack: How to Approach the Animation of a Crazy Video Game
description: Join Highball Animation in collaboration with Undercoders for a look at how we brought Denshattack to life. We’re going to walk you through our animation process, starting with how we move from first sketches to the final high-speed polish without losing efficiency. We’ll also share our favorite camera tricks and show you how we use framing to make the game feel snappy and responsive for the player. Finally, we’ll talk about our approach to cinematics and how we mix different styles and techniques to make every moment of the game stand out. Come see how we combine a solid workflow with creativity to make even our wildest ideas work.

title: SAFER by Design: Rewriting the rule book
description: What if ‘secure by design’ was just the beginning, but not nearly enough? In today’s fast-paced world, where design decisions can affect millions, safety must be our guiding principle. In this high-tempo keynote, Sarah Armstrong-Smith introduces the SAFER by design framework: Secure, Accessible, Functional, Environmental, Reliable. This isn’t a checklist – SAFER represents a transformative mindset shift for everyone shaping the future of tech, from engineers to execs. Attendees will gain actionable insights to embed SAFER principles into every stage of product development, driving innovation and resilience. By embracing SAFER by design, we raise the bar for industry standards and deliver lasting positive change.

title: From Prompt to Production: Rapid Prototyping of AI Applications with Firebase Studio
description: Turn a natural language prompt into a production-ready AI web application, live. This session shows how the Firebase Studio Application Prototyping agent generates the initial structure of a complete Next.js project, connects it to Firebase services, and uses the Gemini-powered Code Assist to perform instant refinements, testing, and one-click deployments. You’ll be ready to launch your own idea in minutes, all from the browser.

title: Our Roadmap to Safe AI
description: As AI models become increasingly capable, the need to ensure that these models are aligned with our objectives and that their behaviour does not contravene our fundamental norms becomes more and more crucial. With increasing frequency, we are seeing cases of LLMs dispaying unwanted behaviours, such as deception, excessive sycophancy and self-preservation. At LawZero, we are exploring and developing alternative methodologies to current LLM structures in order to train advanced AI models that are more transparent, reliable and aligned. We want to dispel the myth that increased AI safety can only come at the expense of advancing capabilities.

title: AI + Human Judgment: How to Make HR More Efficient Without Losing Its Soul
description: 1. Narcis Berenguer: Manager at Marigold (moderator)2. Miquel Mora: CEO at Tauniqo3. Juan José Jiménez: Director at Robert Walters4. Georgina Ferri: General Director at BetevéShowcase real and practical examples of how AI is already supporting HR teams. Break the fear of AI as a replacement, positioning it as an enabler. Reposition HR as a strategic partner rather than an administrative function. Provide actionable takeaways that attendees can apply in their organizations.

title: The Code of the Future: What Companies Look for in AI Talent?
description: 1. M. José Jiménez Jiménez – Vice-rector for Teaching and Students – Universitat Politècnica de Catalunya – BarcelonaTech (UPC) – Universitat Politècnica de Catalunya (Moderator)2. Ernest Teniente López – Director of inLab FIB (Barcelona School of Informatics)3. Mercè Vila Albacete – Recent graduate of the Master’s degree in Statistics and Operations Research (MESIO UPC-UB) and Data Science Area Manager at inLab FIB en UPC4. Natalia Martínez Echarte – Director of Culture and Talent at SEIDOR5. Karen Oliveros Felez – Data Architect at Siemens Energy, with a Master’s in AI at Siemens EnergyBeyond the code: what do companies and institutions really seek in AI professionals? Academic and business experts will discuss “hybrid competencies”— technical skills and human-centric soft skills—ethics, and adaptability in a disruptive landscape. We will discover how UPC shapes new profiles to meet global challenges and how young talent can lead digital transformation through critical thinking and creativity applied to technology.

title: Humanizing Technology at Seidor
description: 1. Iván González Álvarez – Global Director of Marketing, Communication & Sustainability at Seidor2. Natalia Martínez Echarte – Director of Culture and Talent at Seidor

title: Decoded: Finding Your Superpower in Cybersecurity
description: 1. Clarisa Tavolieri – Global Head of Cyber Data Management at Zurich Technology Delivery Center Barcelona2. Yosune Reguera (Moderator) – Technical Program Manager, Digital Development at Zurich Technology Delivery Center Barcelona3. Mora Durante Astrada – Global Head of Security Education & Awareness at Zurich Technology Delivery Center Barcelona4. Teresa Di Punzio – Information Security Consultant at Zurich Technology Delivery Center BarcelonaCybersecurity isn’t just for hackers — it’s for humans with curiosity, creativity, and courage. Three women from diverse paths share how data, awareness, and business insight turned into their superpowers in one of tech’s fastest-growing fields. Discover how your skills could open doors in the world of digital trust.

title: Why soft skills are becoming increasingly important in a digital-first world
description: 1. Lea Hauser – Agile Team Coach at Porsche Digital2. Tahira Farid – Software Engineer at Porsche Digital3. Islam Fahmi – Software Engineer at Porsche DigitalAs technology, systems, and automation accelerate execution, the real challenge shifts from tools to people. This session explores why communication, decision-making, trust, and alignment have become the hardest and most critical skills for leaders and teams in a digital-first environment.

title: Sensitive Robots for Industry
description: The demonstration will feature a quadruped robot equipped with control software and artificial intelligence, capable of responding to interactions with the environment and humans, both in a directed and autonomous manner.

title: Decode Yourself: Discover Your DISC Style and Improve How You Work
description: 1. Javier Martínez – Leader – Claire Joster Technology at Claire Joster2. José María Flores – Regional Leader – Madrid at Claire Joster3. Jordi Valls – Senior Consultant 360 at Claire Joster4.Jordi Llahí – Sales and Marketing Leader at Claire Joster5. Gemma Solis – Senior Consultant at Claire JosterThe DISC model identifies four behavioral styles that influence how we make decisions, communicate, and face challenges. Understanding them improves teamwork, professional effectiveness, and interpersonal relationships, helping individuals gain better self-awareness and respond appropriately in any situation. A workshop designed to help participants decode themselves through DISC.

title: Explore AI like a start up. Scale it like a global powerhouse.
description: In this session, we’ll explore how Nestlé approaches artificial intelligence with the agility of a start up while scaling it with the strength and reliability of a global powerhouse. We’ll show how we rapidly test ideas and turn early learnings into robust solutions that can scale across markets and teams.

title: IKIGAI: The art of following your purpose in the age of AI
description: Francesc Miralles, co-author of the groundbreaking book praised by Oprah Winfrey, translated into 70 languages with 6 million copies sold, will talk about the purpose in work and life according to this fascinating and inspiring Japanese concept. To do so, we’ll learn how the four circles of ikigai work, as well as the tools we have at our disposal to discover and enhance our life’s mission in the age of AI.

title: Blockchain for Social Impact: Empowering Diversity and Promoting Sustainability
description: 1. Paris Dufrayer – WiBT – Women in Blockchain Talks – Barcelona Chapter Leader2. Lavinia Osbourne – WiBT – Women in Blockchain Talks – Founder & Thought Leader3. Tiffani Byrd – WiBT & SettleMint – Head of Partnerships (Asset Tokenization & Digital Assets)This workshop empowers participants to understand blockchain and AI concepts for social impact, review real use cases, identify opportunities in inclusion, sustainability, and innovation, and create group frameworks for impactful solutions. It also covers Tokens, NFTs, and POAPs, and each attendee will receive one POAP. This session is interactive and fun.

title: Live Hacking: 45 Minutes to Break Into a System
description: With Mario Álvarez from the El Pingüino de Mario channel, we’ll experience a real ethical hacking session. In just 45 minutes, we’ll carry out an attack in a controlled environment to understand how attackers think. The goal is purely educational and defensive: learning to identify vulnerabilities before it’s too late. A practical, must-see demo to strengthen our defenses by anticipating threats.

title: Observability for Next‑Generation Digital Ecosystems
description: 1. David Sánchez Carmona – Senior Salesforce Engineer at Dynatrace2. Dani Coll – Senior Developer Advocate at Dynatrace3. Isidre Royo Bonnin – Senior Product Manager at DynatraceIn a world of exponential complexity, applications and Agentic everywhere, discover how Dynatrace delivers monitoring and advanced observability across modern digital ecosystems. Senior Developers will learn about data ingestion, topology and end‑to‑end tracing to link every transaction to its underlying infrastructure. A deep Salesforce cloud platform visibility—from security events and system logs to KPIs to operate resilient platforms.

title: Aubac: Gamify the modification of files
description: This talk presents Aubac, an experimental video game that transforms the player’s file system into the primary space for interaction. Through the use of APIs like System.IO, real-time change detection, and data manipulation, the game transforms everyday actions such as renaming, moving, or modifying files into playable mechanics. The technical decisions behind this approach will be explored, along with the challenges of working with real files.

title: From Earth to the Moon with Hope: How Resilience and Artificial Intelligence Can Change Everything
description: What if innovation sprang from effort and curiosity, even from home with just a simple laptop? In this inspiring talk, mining engineer and the only international winner of NASA’s lunar challenge, María Jesús Puerta, shares the story of Esperanza: a digital twin capable of recycling waste on the Moon. A true story showing that effort and technology can take us farther than we ever imagined.

title: Programming is dead. Long live programming!
description: Programming isn’t dying, it’s just transforming. AI has arrived to stay, shaking the foundations of software development and opening up a range of opportunities and new technologies. Smarter web applications, LLMs running in your browser, new APIs like WebGPU to harness your GPU, and unprecedented ways to elevate the user experience. All with live examples. Because knowing how to program is what will make the difference between creating amazing things or blindly trusting that AI is never wrong. (Spoiler: it is.)

title: Women and Digital Skills: The Role of Barcelona Activa in Technological Empowerment
description: 1. Maria Eugènia Marín Mínguez (moderator) – Equality Officer at Barcelona Activa2. Laia M. Lerma – Teach Lead at Alumni IT Academy3. Laura Copete Trujillo – Senior Digital Talent Technician at Barcelona Activa4. Stella Mally – Lawyer specializing in social compliance, inclusive leadership, gender equality, and responsible people management

title: Designing architecture with EventStorming and Bounded Contexts
description: Are you tired of being blocked by other teams or working in a large, shared codebase where every change feels risky? Learn how to evolve a tangled monolith—or a distributed monolith—into a clean, scalable distributed architecture. This talk will show you how to identify Bounded Contexts using a Big Picture EventStorming workshop, in close collaboration with Domain Experts, to uncover boundaries between subsystems.

title: Meetup with REDTEAMING
description: 1. Jae Yeol (Jerry) Kim – Research Fellow in Future Tech & Strategy at LG Uplus | Vice Exec Chair at 6G Forum at LG U+2. Michael Hwang – Co-Founder and Chief Strategy Officer at Datumo

title: How do you start building a Telco LLM and what pitfalls to look for
From Expert to Leader: Growing a Sustainable Career in Tech
description: 1. Iker Ganuza (moderator) – VP West Europe – Strategy & Transformation and Spain Digital Hubs Lead2. Andrzej Danielewicz – Global Head of Data & AI Products at Pepsico3. Ana Sotelo – Technical Data Product Director at Pepsico4. Igor González – Iberia People Director at PepsicoCareer growth in tech is no longer one-dimensional. This panel explores different career path, from individual contributor to tech leader, and what leadership looks like in today’s engineering and product teams. Panelists will share personal experiences, lessons learned, and how organizations can support continuous development.

title: Micro-Frontend & Me, a love story
description: I always thought Micro-frontends were stupid, unproven tech, and a fad. After speaking with industry leaders on my podcast about how they implemented it at scale, I realized that it might actually solve some issues we are having in our company. This talk focuses on the journey of implementing micro-frontends, from trade-offs, implementation, and choosing the right tools for the job.

title: Artificial Intelligence: Wave or Tsunami
description: Jon Hernández analyzes how AI is dismantling traditional competitive moats. This session explores the paradigm shift redefining the labor market and the urgent need for adaptation. Learn to move beyond the hype and integrate AI as a strategic co-pilot. Key Takeaways: The impact of AI on market agility. Redefining productivity in the digital era. Frameworks for effective technology adoption.

title: Enterprise Spec Driven Development
description: In today’s large-scale and highly distributed enterprise environments, organizations must build software that is consistent, scalable, and tightly aligned with business rules and operational objectives. Enterprise Spec Driven Development introduces a strategic approach in which formal, evolving, and shared specifications become the single source of truth guiding the design, development, and lifecycle of modern enterprise solutions.

title: Immersive Interview Experience: Live a Real Interview
description: 1. Javier Martínez – Leader – Claire Joster Technology at Claire Joster2. José María Flores – Regional Leader – Madrid at Claire Joster3. Jordi Valls – Senior Consultant 360 at Claire Joster4.Jordi Llahí – Sales and Marketing Leader at Claire JosterThe job interview is a demanding process that requires preparation and strategy. Through a hands-on, “training mode” experience, participants can practice, improve their pitch, and gain confidence to perform at their best in a real interview. In this workshop, you will find the most realistic simulator to help you avoid mistakes in an actual job interview.

title: Sensitive Robots for Industry
description: The demonstration will feature a quadruped robot equipped with control software and artificial intelligence, capable of responding to interactions with the environment and humans, both in a directed and autonomous manner.

title: The New Travel Project
description: Travel is one of the most desired experiences and is undergoing transformation thanks to technology and Digital Experience Design. With the customer at the center and personalized phygital experiences, World2Meet will present in this keynote its vision and The New Travel Project, aimed at redefining the design, marketing, and travel experience, generating value for travelers, partners, and destinations.

title: Global Semiconductor Talent Development and International Collaboration
description: Global semiconductor talent development is increasingly vital as supply chains globalize and technology cycles accelerate. This abstract examines the global talent history and future strategies for building a skilled workforce through education, industry–academia partnerships and international collaboration which enables knowledge exchange while addressing talent shortages across regions.

title: Presentation of Google Developer Groups Spain
Cybersecurity: The Gateway to Your Career in the Digital Sector
description: 1. Daniel Sánchez – Mentor at IT Academy2. Jordi Gay – CEO at HRScience3. Juan Carlo Castillo – Mentor at IT Academy4. Javier Dominguez – CEO at PUEA hands-on introductory cybersecurity workshop designed to spark digital vocations. Using Packet Tracer, we will dynamically explore how networks work, identify risks, and apply basic defense concepts. An accessible, motivating, and 100% practical experience to take the first step in the tech sector.

title: Prompt Battle: Master AI
description: 1. Anna Sallés – Data Scientist at Caixabank2. Francesc Albareda – Data Scientist at Caixabank3. Ignasi Fibla – Data Scientist at Caixabank4. Jordi Geli – Technical Solutions at Caixabank5. Carmen Martín – Data Scientist at CaixabankAfter a brief and dynamic introduction to LLMs and their key uses, you will face several challenges designed to test your ingenuity and creativity, competing with other participants and showing your ability to go beyond the machine. Ready to take on the challenge? Get ready to challenge AI, amaze your audience, and remember that human creativity still makes the difference.

title: Beyond the Model: Agentic Orchestration as the New AI Architecture
description: This session examines the shift from standalone models to orchestrated multi-agent systems. We explore how structured memory, task decomposition, and small specialized models redefine scalability and autonomy. Designed for leaders, it provides a strategic framework to understand how agentic systems impact enterprise architecture, operational efficiency, and long-term competitiveness in the new era of AI deployments.SPONSORED BY

title: From Hype to Harmony: Implementing Agentic AI Responsibly At Scale
description: AI agents are transforming work at Zurich—making processes faster, smarter, and more efficient. From automating tasks to enabling complex decisions, they deliver real value. The next wave, Agentic AI, brings autonomy and big opportunities—but also risks. Success means innovation with trust: safety, transparency, and accountability from day one.

title: Semiconductors: The Cornerstone of an AI-Driven Future
description: In this session, I will first review the semiconductor market and the industry’s evolution during the tech paradigm shift. Then, I will introduce the development of AI stacks following the launch of ChatGPT and analyze the dominant forces in the AI ecosystem and their semiconductor footprints. Lastly, I will depict the future world empowered by AI and robots, along with the emerging demand and challenges for semiconductors.

title: From response to preparedness: AI & and Data at the service of people and society
description: 1. Miguel Ángel Lliébanas – RAIGHT.ai – CTO2. Idoia Salazar – OdiseIA – President3. Richard Benjamins – 28DIGITAL – Chairman4. Linus Bengtsson – Flowminder – Chair of the Board & Co-FounderThe world is increasingly suffering from natural disasters attributed to climate change. Frequent flooding is not only happening anymore in the global south, but also in Europe and the US, with the resulting disruption and fatal victims. Today, the fight against such disasters focuses mostly on responding once the disaster has happened (Response). While this is of crucial importance, it is equally important to focus on preparedness. Countries that are prepared can be more efficient in the response phase, reducing the amount of fatal victims, as well as the economic and societal disruption.In this panel, we will discuss how to be prepared for natural disasters, how AI & data plays a key role, and what challenges hinder preparedness. The panel will also show concrete and real examples of preparedness, provide an open source data platform, and announce the Mobile Resilient Initiative, a global community for preparedness.

title: Innovative and Technological Talent: The Key Role of Vocational Education and Training
description: 1. Antonio Saravia – Deputy Director for the Digital Economy at Red.es (Moderator)2. Emilio Cabanes – Deputy Director of CRN E-commerce and Digital Marketing for Vocational Training at EOI3. Alejandra Blázquez – Head of Cisco Networking Academy at CISCO4. Mª Paz Sánchez – Director General of Planning, Innovation, and Management of Vocational Training at the Ministry of Education, Vocational Training and SportsThis panel will examine how, in a labor market that demands profiles capable of driving the digital and green transition, vocational education and training (VET) educates over 1,200,000 students with programs aligned with the productive sector. It will explain how, thanks to collaboration among stakeholders and public-private initiatives, key needs and profiles are identified. Today, VET is the main source of talent for many technology companies.SPONSORED BY

title: Catalyst Pharma: Where Tech & Digital Talent Shapes Global Impact
description: 1. Jordi Pérez Conill – Head of Global IT Network Operations Center at Boehringer Ingelheim2. Carlos Fernando Da Silva – Head of IT Operations & EHS at Boehringer Ingelheim3. Arantxa Juan Hernández – Team Lead AI & Automation at Boehringer Ingelheim4. Ana Lucía García Ortin – Head of IT in Animal Health at Boehringer IngelheimWhat does a people‑first global tech hub look like inside pharma? This roundtable offers a look inside IT & Digital Services hubs at Boehringer Ingelheim, highlighting our open culture, flexible work, and diverse teams. We’ll discuss how tech and digital talent shapes projects with global impact in the pharma sector, and what a people-first tech hub means in practice.

title: Hack Your CV: Key Tips to Optimize Your CV and Beat AI Filters
description: 1. Carlos Moratal – Regional Leader Claire Joster – Cataluña y Aragón at Claire Joster2. Maitane Urizarbarrena – People Partner at Eurofirms3. Katia Molero – Senior Consultant 360 at Claire JosterIn today’s recruitment processes, many résumés are automatically filtered before reaching human hands. Understanding how these systems work is key to improving job opportunities. This workshop provides practical tools and strategies to adapt your CV to AI filters, aiming especially to empower unemployed individuals, people with diverse profiles, or those in vulnerable situations.

title: The Missing Structure in Most Tech Teams: Building a Strong Product Trio
description: Tech teams are expected to move fast and act autonomously. But when Product, Design and Tech don’t truly operate as a unit, delivery slows down and decisions get revisited. The Product Trio isn’t just a discovery practice — it’s a structural choice that impacts speed, clarity and real autonomy. In this roundtable, we’ll discuss about what changes when the trio is strong — and what quietly breaks when it isn’t.

title: Gaming, Language and Communities
description: A conversation between content creator LopezNorman44 and video game developer Unai Estavillo from Coolom Games (developers of Dating Killmulator) about building communities in the digital environment around video games.

title: What can I do to convince you to get a job in cybersecurity?
description: Why do so many talented people excel in international cybersecurity competitions, yet never choose a career in cybersecurity? Is it because they don’t apply – or because jobs aren’t attractive enough? Or are we trapped by outdated ideas about who these roles are for and what skills they require?This session challenges the myths behind the cybersecurity skills shortage and shows how cybersecurity can become an opportunity for professionals far beyond IT.

title: AI Changed the Rules for Teamwork – Are You Ready?
description: AI writes code in parallel, discovers and fixes problems in production, and incorporates customer feedback in real-time. Using agentic AI in software development is not just adding a new tool to your workflow like Git or CI/CD; it redefines how developers get work done. If agents step in as our technical advisors, coaches, and strategic partners, how will humans work together? Do we still need traditional teams? Will we plan our work together? Is this the end of the cross-functional team? Let’s throw out the crystal ball and see what’s possible today and how teams are already transforming their ways of working. One thing is clear: we must recalibrate how we work together.

title: Sensitive Robots for Industry
description: The demonstration will feature a quadruped robot equipped with control software and artificial intelligence, capable of responding to interactions with the environment and humans, both in a directed and autonomous manner.

title: FP and Digital Transformation: Global Opportunities with Real Impact
description: 1. Mari Carmen Moneva Montero – Chief Human Resources Officer at Indra Group2. Sebastian Bamonde Bermudez de Castro – Chief Operations Officer, Tech at Indra Group3. Maria Fernanda Silles – Director of Talent Acquisition and Internal Mobility at Indra GroupVocational Education as a Driver of Innovation and Employment. The Strategic Businesses and Areas of Indra Group. How We Lead Digital Transformation in Key Sectors. Opportunities for Young People Who Want to Transform the World.

title: AI in a supercomputing world: the next scientific frontier
description: Artificial Intelligence and High-Performance Computing are converging to redefine the way science is conceived, executed, and applied. This fusion is enabling breakthroughs across domains: from building Earth Twins to understand and mitigate climate change, to developing sovereign chip designs tailored for AI, and advancing dual-use technologies that balance innovation with responsibility. European Large Language Models are reshaping how multilingual societies engage with knowledge, while new computational methods are opening frontiers in social sciences, medicine, and engineering alike. This new era is not just accelerating discovery; it is evolving the very scientific method toward a future where AI-driven simulations, transparent data practices, and ethical design are central pillars of progress. In this talk, we will do a walkthrough all these topics with sparks from the work that the Barcelona Supercomputing Center has been leading wordlwide

title: Barcelona as a Global Tech Hub: Inside IFCO & OLX
description: Barcelona is becoming a key location for global tech innovation and talent. AI & Experimentation at OLX We’ll discuss how OLX is leading the AI race in the classifieds industry and how AI is expanding OLX’s experimentation and innovation capabilities. Towards IFCO Semantic Layer By introducing the Semantic Layer, IFCO transforms complex, technology-driven data into trusted entities and KPIs aligned across regions and departments.

title: Renaissance 2.0: Against the Fragmented Mind
description: For centuries, humanity progressed thanks to hybrid minds—those that combined science, art, and philosophy without artificial divisions. Today, in the age of artificial intelligence, we face the same challenge: will we continue to see creativity and logic as separate worlds? AI is blurring these boundaries, and the future belongs to those who embrace the convergence of technology and humanity. It is time to revive the spirit of the Renaissance, where critical thinking, imagination, and technical knowledge merge to create something truly transcendent.

title: Strategic Upskilling: The Engine of Talent Development and Business Competitiveness
description: 1. Juanjo Moreno – Ironhack – Account Manager (Moderador)2. Cristina Troya – T Systems – People Lead3. Jordi Roig – Socotec -Director RRHH4. Cristina Mont – Lavinia – Strategy and Innovation DirectorIn an environment shaped by AI, automation, and constant change, upskilling has become a key lever to attract, retain, and develop talent. Beyond training, it is about building critical capabilities, fostering versatility, and accelerating business adaptation. A strategic approach helps close skills gaps, improve productivity, and strengthen a culture of continuous learning.

title: Princess of Girona Inspires: Building Digital Talent with Impact and Values
description: 1. Elisenda Bou-Balust – Co-Founder & CEO, Cala AI2. Ignasi Belda – Department Director, Banco de España3. Pau García-Milà – Co-founder & Co-CEO, Founderz Business School (Moderator)4. Ingrid Aznar Juncà – Awards Director, Princess of Girona FoundationHow do you build a technological career with real impact? Which decisions make a difference when you’re just starting out? What role do ethics, entrepreneurship, and leadership play in the development of digital talent?In this inspiring conversation, Ignasi Belda, Pau García-Milà, and Elisenda Bou—awardees of the Fundació Princesa de Girona—will share the stage to reflect on how to grow professionally in the tech sector from very diverse perspectives and experiences.Through an open and approachable dialogue, they will address key topics such as starting a career, decision-making in moments of uncertainty, the value of failure, entrepreneurship as a driver of change, responsibility in technological development, and the importance of incorporating ethical and human criteria into innovation.An honest and motivating conversation that aims to convey that there is no single path to success, but there are shared values that can make a difference.

title: CTO Leadership Lab: Communicate, Attract, and Retain Tech Talent
description: 1. Sandra Alonso – Program Leader at Eurofirms2. Marisa Martínez – Diversity & Inclusion Consultant at Eurofirms3. Mireia López – Diversity & Inclusion Consultant at EurofirmsCTOs and technology leaders face the challenge of turning business vision into technical decisions and transforming complexity into execution. Beyond technical expertise, their main challenge is to communicate clearly and effectively with diverse audiences, adapting the message, approach, and communication impact to connect technology, people, and business. The ultimate goal is to turn technological decisions into business actions.

title: Build the ultimate superhero team with AI !
description: Are you ready to create an API that manages a team of superheroes so they can battle against others? Come to our hands-on workshop to assemble your superhero team and see if you can beat your opponents in a mini-league! We will develop an API with Java using Spring Boot and AI and put it to the test against each other 😉

title: From Prompts to Partners: Building Your First AI Agent
description: 1. Carlos García Martín – Engineering Team Leader at Sage2. Pablo Noras – AI ML Engineer at Sage3. Enric Soler Rastrollo – Senior Architect at Sage4. Robert Rodriguez – AI ML Engineer at SageAI is no longer just about asking good questions—it’s about creating systems that can think, act, and collaborate. In this hands-on workshop, you’ll learn what AI agents really are, how they work, and how to design your own. No PhD required. By the end, you’ll understand how agents reason, use tools, and make decisions—and you’ll prototype one yourself. Perfect for builders, product people, founders, and curious minds who want to move beyond ChatGPT and into agentic AI.

title: How to hire 100 people in 24 hours?
description: 1. Fernando Viyella – AI Strategy & Pre‑Sales Lead at Experis2. Fernando Aguilar – Head of Experis Academy at Experis3. Matteo Di Cicco – Strategic Partnership at Experis4. Vicenç Alvaro – Marketing Manager at ExperisExperis has been selected by a banking giant to lead its Cloud Transformation Hub in Spain. The client needs to recruit 100 Junior Fullstack Developers in a record time of 24 hours to avoid delays in the deployment of its new financial infrastructure. The success of this mission depends on your ability to orchestrate IA agents who find, screen, and secure the commitment of this talent without compromising quality.

title: Data & AI: With great power comes great responsibility
description: What makes someone stand out in data or AI? Spoiler: it’s not mastering models, dashboards, or the latest technologies. Curiosity is your greatest asset. Asking better questions and understanding context is what turns data into real knowledge. In this session, we’ll talk about how you can become the kind of professional who helps others make smarter decisions and creates meaningful impact. Because data is cool… but knowledge is the end game.

title: Autonomous Business: Are We Ready for AI to Make Decisions?
description: Autonomous Business is the next paradigm following the digital transformation of organizations. Agentic AI takes a central role and promises efficiency, faster time-to-market, and value creation at levels we never imagined. But for these promises to be fulfilled, companies and organizations must have both their technology and their people prepared. Is yours ready?

title: EU Got Talent: Upskilling & Reskilling Europe’s Future Talent
description: Marjorie Grassler – EU project manager at Mobile World Capital Barcelona

title: Developing technical systems in Unreal Engine for HeartWeaver: Gravity, Narrative, Static Camera and Persistence.
description: While developing HeartWeaver – currently featured in Steam Next Fest with a free demo – our primary challenge was implementing systems that had to be easy to use for non-developers but also scalable and modifiable, since game development is an iterative journey to “find the fun”.In this talk, you will be able to know more about the technical challenges while developing systems that do not come out of the box in Unreal Engine: gravity switch mechanics, multi-format dialogues with localisation, a rail-based static camera system with custom framing and a multi-slot save system.

title: GRDB Students Presentation | Digital Ecosystem BCN
description: 1. Maria Eugenia Laureckis Molla – Lecturer – ESIC2. Guillermina Aloisio Taramasco – ESIC Student3. Victor Daniel Ricciardi Lopez – ESIC Student4. Jan Lucena Podadera – ESIC Student5. Miquel Navas Aviles – ESIC Student

title: 42 Barcelona: From Disruptive Learning to Real Tech Employability
description: In a fast-changing world, talent is defined not only by what we know, but by how we learn and who has access to learning. Ainhoa Zamora, director of 42 Barcelona, presents a model based on autonomy, continuous learning, peer collaboration, and inclusion, accompanied by Laura García, a 42 Barcelona graduate, who shows how this free, global approach creates real tech employability.

title: SEO in the Age of AI: How to Rank in LLMs
description: Carlos Ortega examines the “death of SEO” in the AI era. He explains how ChatGPT and Gemini consume web data and why SEO remains the base for AI answers. Learn how models find your site and how to position your brand in the new search landscape. Key Takeaways: How LLMs crawl your site. Why SEO is vital for AI. Steps for visibility in AI engines.

title: Fireside chat with Steve Aoki
STREAMING Fireside chat with Steve Aoki
Hack Your Interview: Face Interviews with Confidence and Authenticity
description: 1. Sandra Alonso – Program Leader at Eurofirms2. Marisa Martínez – Diversity & Inclusion Consultant at Eurofirms3. Mireia López – Diversity & Inclusion Consultant at EurofirmsIn a tech sector where cognitive diversity drives innovation, approaching interviews with authenticity is key. Neurodiversity adds value to the recruitment process. This workshop provides practical tools for managing interviews and communication with confidence, helping neurodivergent and diverse individuals showcase their talent, strengthen self-awareness, and enter the tech sector with greater assurance.

title: Building with Open-Source AI: From Theory to Practice
description: 1. Alberto Gago Fernández – Managing Director at Spain Agency for the Supervision of Artificial Intelligence2. Antonio J. Jara – CTO at Libelium3. Alicia Asín – CEO & Co-founder at LibeliumAre you bored with AI talks that only focus on trends? Discover the initiative of the MTDFP Open Source AI Community, showcasing the technical aspects of real-world use cases. Solutions to concrete challenges and applications with social impact. You’ll see how the community is collaborating to solve real problems using open tools, and how you can get involved, collaborate, and contribute. Come to this talk if you want to be part of the change.

title: AI and Europe’s Digital Destiny: Building Sovereignty through Values
description: As artificial intelligence becomes the backbone of the digital century, Europe faces a decisive choice: allow its sovereignty to erode or build it on its own terms. By federating AI infrastructure, securing control over data and compute, and embedding safety, sustainability, and human dignity into technology itself, we can turn values into strategic advantage. This keynote calls for a Europe that leads not through domination, but through example, positioning trust, collaboration, and environmental responsibility as the foundations of a sovereign European AI ecosystem.

title: New Quantum Materials for Quantum 2.0
description: The birth of Quantum Physics in the early 20th century gave rise to all modern technology. This first “Quantum Revolution” made use of the most basic concepts in quantum physics. In the coming decades, a second quantum revolution (Quantum 2.0), using much more advanced quantum physics, will dramatically change the way we do science and technology, with dramatic implications for the economy and national security. In his talk, Prof. Jarillo-Herrero will discuss a new generation of quantum materials with the potential to revolutionize quantum simulations, quantum sensing, and AI applications

title: It’s not just another bot: it’s an inspirational agent for the shopping of the future
description: The next generation of shopping will go beyond web and apps: it will live in an interoperable agentic ecosystem. We introduce Vueling’s Inspirational Agent, an agentic architecture with MCPs and MCPApps, integrable into owned channels or external environments. We explain context design, coordination, and tool-calling over core systems, ensuring observability, security, governance, and controlled costs—laying the foundation today for the agentic world of tomorrow.

title: Onboarding Claude Code: from babysitting to autonomous agents
description: A 90-day onboarding playbook for an AI coding agent in real-world repos: complex domain logic, legacy migrations, low test coverage, outdated docs. From day-one setup – where you review every line – through guardrails and context management, to autonomous agents that can own your repo while you tackle the problems no one’s solved yet.

title: Talent, AI, and Productivity in Software Engineering
description: Luis Delgado Schwarzmann Jimenez – Managing Director at CaixaBank Tech

title: Closing the Velocity Gap: Syncing AI and Innovation with Healthcare Adoption
description: 1. Nicole Arbiv – Strategic Programs Leader at Roche (Moderator)2. Alex Morcillo – Senior Manager IT Products Architecture at Roche3. Laura Vidal Borrell – Global Director Portfolio and Product Management at RocheAI and digital technologies are advancing at unprecedented speed, yet healthcare adoption often lags due to regulatory, cultural, and operational constraints. This talk explores the “velocity gap” between innovation and real-world impact, drawing on experience from Roche to share practical insights on governance, cross-functional collaboration, and execution strategies that turn AI into sustainable value for patients and healthcare professionals

title: Data, Governance, and Technology as Key Ingredients
description: 1. Guillermo Prieto – Director of Information Technologies2. Albert Del Baño – Head of IT Operations3. Adriana López-Doriga Guerra – Data Architect at NennisiwokDamm drives a Data and AI strategy based on the IAmm Hub, created to accelerate projects and generate value. Its key pillars are Data Governance, essential for ensuring quality and security, and a flexible, robust technology platform. The talk will showcase the current status, key learnings, and challenges in scaling a reliable and adaptable ecosystem.

title: Creating a Real Product with AI: Professional Vibe Coding for Developers
description: In this workshop we will build a functional product from scratch, using artificial intelligence tools as real engineering accelerators.This is not about asking a model for code and copying it. It’s about designing, deciding, and building better.During the session we will work with tools such as Stitch to structure and rapidly prototype the experience, Claude Code and Open Code to iterate on architecture and business logic, and Antigravity to explore agent-assisted workflows. We will also see how to run models directly in the browser using ONNX, integrating AI capabilities into the product itself without relying exclusively on the backend.Throughout the process we will cover:Defining architecture with AI support without delegating technical judgmentValidating and refactoring model-generated codeDesigning AI-powered user experiencesIntegrating models in the frontend and understanding their real limitationsWhat changes in the role of the senior developer when code generation is almost instantaneous?AI does not replace the developer. It multiplies them — but only if they know how to use it.This workshop is aimed at experienced developers who want to understand how to integrate AI into their professional workflow, improve their delivery capacity, and redefine their value in the era of intelligent assistants.SPONSORED BY

title: Talent and Disability: Breaking Barriers and Driving Inclusion in the AI Era
description: 1. Sílvia Sousa Cristòfol – Strategy and Transformation Director at SEIDOR / Directora d’Estratègia i Transformació a SEIDOR2. Javier Pascual Gurpegui – People, Organization and e-Administration Manager at Ajuntament de Barcelona / Gerent de Persones, Organització i Administració Electrònica a l’Ajuntament de Barcelona3. Ernest Pla Martínez – Global VP of Talent & Organization at Grifols / Vicepresident Global de Talent i Organització a Grifols4. Albert Campabadal – President at Grupo SIFU / President a Grupo SIFU5. Sandra Alonso – Program Leader at Eurofirms Foundation / Responsable de Programa a Eurofirms FoundationJoin this inspiring and co-creative session where we will bring together people with disabilities, talent teams, and experts from various organizations to explore how to drive inclusion and break down barriers to employment. In a hands-on session, with inspiring testimonies and collaborative activities, we will design solutions to enhance inclusion and amplify abilities through technology. Come help humanize the future!

title: Build Your First AI Model in Under 60 Minutes – From Zero to Deployed
description: 1. Louis Moreau – Head of Developer Relations at Edge Impulse2. Jim Bruges – Senior Developer Relations Engineer at Edge Impulse3. Marc Pous – Developer Relations Staff Engineer at Edge ImpulseJoin us for a hands-on workshop where you’ll learn how to train and deploy your first Machine Learning model using Edge Impulse. Designed for beginners and experienced developers, the session covers data collection, model training, and deployment on edge devices. You’ll build an end-to-end real-time Object Detection application running directly on hardware. No prior ML experience required—just bring your laptop and curiosity!

title: Building complex code-defined AI agents that execute robotic actions
description: In this session, we will break open the black box of language model–based agents (LLMs) to understand how they are built and connected. We will also explore how these agents can go beyond simply answering questions with text, performing real-world actions that are automatically executed by a robotic arm.

title: Quantum Sensors: The Technology That Measures the Impossible
description: When people talk about quantum technology, almost all the attention goes to quantum computers, presented as the great revolution of the future. But they are not the only one, nor necessarily the most influential. This talk focuses on a silent revolution already underway: quantum sensors. Through atomic clocks and SQUIDs, it combines scientific explanation with real-world anecdotes to show how quantum physics has moved from theory to measuring the impossible.

title: Another bot? No: an architecture
description: As demand for chatbots grows, creating isolated assistants becomes unsustainable. Our solution is an agent‑based architecture with a single entry point, MIA, which autonomously determines what each user can access based on their profile. By orchestrating specialized agents, MIA ensures scalability, consistency, and a more controlled, efficient user experience.

title: Immersive Interview Experience: Live a Real Interview
description: 1. Javier Martínez – Leader – Claire Joster Technology at Claire Joster2. José María Flores – Regional Leader – Madrid at Claire Joster3. Jordi Valls – Senior Consultant 360 at Claire Joster4.Jordi Llahí – Sales and Marketing Leader at Claire JosterThe job interview is a demanding process that requires preparation and strategy. Through a hands-on, “training mode” experience, participants can practice, improve their pitch, and gain confidence to perform at their best in a real interview. In this workshop, you will find the most realistic simulator to help you avoid mistakes in an actual job interview.

title: Do’s & Don’ts for your Indie game
description: Lessons learned from our latest title: Tiny Lands 2.

title: Learning at the Speed of AI: Upskilling and the Future of Learning
description: The evolving role of upskilling in a landscape where traditional knowledge providers (universities, training companies) coexist with self-service models, pre-packaged courses, and AI-powered learning assistants.

title: From Measurement to Intelligence: Server-Side Strategy in the Cookieless Era
description: How can you personalize at scale without compromising customer trust? MediaMarkt will show how their server-side strategy allows them to control, protect, and activate first-party data to drive AI, secure personalization, and relevant real-time experiences. A hands-on session to understand what it truly means to be data-driven today, shift your mindset, and leave with a clear vision for designing strategies ready for the digital future.

title: How the age of AI and Cloud reshapes prototyping skills
description: Compared to a few years ago, developing applications is cheaper and more “commoditized”. We have programming languages with strong ecosystems of libraries, normalized the use of cloud, converged to sharing AI algoritms as a service, and exploited generative AI to save coding time. This changes how companies develop products and reshapes skills like prototyping. This talk reviews implications and examples at the technical and organizational levels.

title: Shape Your Future: Explore Sage’s Graduate Programme
description: 1. Julie Boulanger – Learning Experience Specialist at Sage2. Blanca Miguel Casanovas – UX Designer at Sage

title: Women influencing and leading the forefront of tech
description: Marjorie Grassler – EU Project Manager at Mobile World Capital Barcelona (Moderator)Alba Cervera Lierta – Established Researcher (Ramón y Cajal Fellow) at Barcelona Supercomputing CenterMarta Portalés Oliva – Head of European Projects at Mobile World Capital BarcelonaEstel Blay – Program Manager at IEEC & Scientist at Hypatia Mars AssociationAs part of Talent Arena, this session brings together three women awarded the DonaTIC Awards, recognized for driving digital talent and paving the way in the STEM ecosystem: Marta Portalés (MWCapital), Alba Cervera Lierta (BSC), and Estel Blay (Associació Hypatia Mars), moderated by Marjorie Grassler (MWCapital). It’s an inspirational conversation that highlights real role models who make an impact and create opportunities for others to succeed. The panel explores how to build a STEM career while navigating data, decisions, and reasonable doubts. The speakers will share the driving forces behind their progress: influences, alliances, and key moments, and how this legacy has translated into innovation, leadership, and new ways of working. The session closes with two simple yet powerful questions: what can you do with this tomorrow? And, above all, who do you want to influence and help grow from now on?

title: The AI-Ready Workforce: Who Thrives, Who Survives?
description: 1. Marcela Mogilska – Founder & Managing Director at Radia (moderator)2. Rosario Sierra – Talent Solutions Leader at LinkedIn Iberia3. Irene Rodríguez Puig – Chief People Officer at Paack4. Iulia Crai – Head of Talent Acquisition at Superhuman5. Andreea Lungulescu – The Principal Recruiter / Talent Crunch – Berlin FounderAs AI reshapes the future of work, companies face new challenges in hiring, developing, and empowering talent. This panel explores how automation will redefine roles, which skills will matter most, and how organizations can adapt to stay competitive. Join us to discover what it takes to thrive in the AI-driven workplace of tomorrow.

title: What If AI Fails?: A Method for Useful and Human-Centered AI
From Idea to Product: Rethinking How We Build in the Age of AI
description: Technology has fundamentally changed almost every part of how we work today. Yet many companies haven’t updated their ways of working. So just how should companies, leaders, and individuals reorganize for this new world?

title: UAB-Cruïlla Chair Robotic Prototypes
description: In the context of the Chair UAB-Cruïlla, it’s talent program is presenting you 2 robotic prototypes, developed within the context of AI in the Music & Arts field. These projects have been developed and will be presented by the students themselves. They are both creating a strat-up. In addition to helping the students to level up their TRL, the Chair provided a real-life scenario to validate their MPV.

title: Authorship, Identity, and Fan Culture in the AI Era
description: This workshop offers digital leaders and creative professionals an ethical, clear, and practical framework to interpret authorship, ownership, and visual identity in the era of generative AI. We examine who can claim rights over AI-generated content, how AI is transforming current creative processes, and how users generate fan-made images and products inspired by brands and cultural icons. We also address how artists and companies can protect their visual identity without stifling new forms of community-driven creativity. Attendees will gain strategic criteria to manage risks, rights, and cultural integrity.

title: Giving AI Agents Superpowers: Connecting Them to the Real World with MCP
description: 1. Darragh Hoare – Graduate AI Engineer at Sage2. Lara Ozdamar – Graduate AI Engineer at Sage3. Daniel Seara – Software Engineer Team Leader at Sage4. Adria Avellaneda – Software Engineer Team Lead at SageAI agents become truly powerful when they can safely interact with real systems—files, databases, APIs, and internal tools. In this workshop, you’ll learn how the Model Context Protocol (MCP) turns agents into first-class operators inside your ecosystem. We’ll demystify MCP, show why it matters, and build agents that can actually do things, not just talk about them.

title: The Red Team: The Telco AI Prompt Engineering Challenge – WIn Prizes!
description: 1. Jerry Jae Yeol Kim – Research Fellow in Future Tech & Strategy at LG Uplus | Vice Exec Chair at 6G Forum at LG U+2. John David Kim – AI Industry Lead, GSMA APACHead of North East Asia & DX Acceleration Forum at GSMA3. Louis Powell – Director of AI Initiatives at GSMA4.
