title: Women Talent in the Tech Sector
description: 1. Eva Blanco (moderator) – Senior advisor culture & employee experience2. Cristina Tomás – Project Manager at Imbee3. Ariadna Masallera – Head of Talent Sopra Steria España at Sopra Steria4. Carla Antunez – Chief of People Officer at Between TechnologyThe tech sector offers opportunities for women talent to grow. This discussion focuses on programs that open doors to new careers, communities that connect women with role models and real support, and companies driving transformative initiatives. Through experiences and best practices, it will show how to inspire vocations, strengthen careers, and foster a more diverse and future-ready tech sector.

title: PLAI – Data and AI Democratization at Sanofi
description: 1. Christian Prieto – Franchise Head Immunology en Sanofi (moderator)2. Jaime Zornoza – Director of Innovation Aily Labs3. Lucas Gallardo – Omnichannel Manager Expert in AI en Sanofi4. Mariam El Akal – Analytics Business Analyst en SanofiMeet PLAI, Sanofi’s decision intelligence platform that turns complex data into snackable, real time insights across R&D, Clinical Ops, M&S, Quality, Finance and GTM. With AI Agents—including Super Agent—PLAI helps teams anticipate, simulate what ifs, and act with confidence. Discover how human + AI collaboration is cutting noise, speeding decisions, and opening a new era of intelligent operations.

title: The community that changed your life (or the one you’re still looking for)
description: 1. Isabel Rodríguez Olivar – Business Development at Next Digital2. Laura Vignali – Event Lead at Next Digital

title: Hard-Earned Truths About Shipping GenAI on the Factory Floor
description: In this talk I’ll share the hard-earned truths from applying GenAI in production for manufacturing at HP: where it actually delivers value, where it breaks (data, integration, latency, reliability, safety/compliance, culture and adoption), and what we changed in our approach to make it usable for operators and engineers.

title: Decode Yourself: Discover Your DISC Style and Improve How You Work
description: 1. Javier Martínez – Leader – Claire Joster Technology en Claire Joster2. José María Flores – Regional Leader – Madrid en Claire Joster3. Jordi Valls – Senior Consultant 360 en Claire Joster4. Jordi Llahí – Sales and Marketing Leader en Claire Joster5. Gemma Solis – Senior Consultant en Claire JosterThe DISC model identifies four behavioral styles that influence how we make decisions, communicate, and face challenges. Understanding them improves teamwork, professional effectiveness, and interpersonal relationships, helping individuals gain better self-awareness and respond appropriately in any situation. A workshop designed to help participants decode themselves through DISC.

title: Fireside Chat: Tim Berners-Lee
Python on Android – How to combine the uncombinable?
description: Follow me on three ways how to integrate a python hardware security module library into your Android app. I) localhost and Termux (🦅🪹) II) translate™️ Python to Kotlin. (😱😱) III) Chaquopy (👀) After scaring the audience with the first two options, I will present the easiest: Chaquopy. Expect an eccentric mix of Python and Kotlin, all to make the most secure Android App possible.

title: Everyday objects as game mechanics
description: Some of the most memorable game mechanics are inspired by the simplest things around us.

title: From Data to Impact: How AI @Roche Is Rewriting Healthcare
description: AI is no longer a future promise in healthcare, it’s driving transformation today. At Roche, AI is being embedded across the healthcare value chain, turning complex data into actionable clinical and operational impact. This keynote shares real-world use cases, lessons learned, and the organizational mindset needed to scale AI from experimentation to measurable outcomes.

title: Engineering in the Age of AI – Why Fundamentals Matter More Than Ever
description: Artificial Intelligence is reshaping how software is built and learned. While AI boosts productivity, it also brings risks like shallow understanding and over-reliance. In this session, Porsche Digital engineers share how to use AI as a co-pilot, where it adds value, and why strong fundamentals remain essential.

title: Discover In-Demand Profiles and Connect with Companies
description: 1. Rosaura Alastruey (moderator) – Networking Facilitator at Rosaura Alastruey and Barcelona Activa2. Maria Barragan – Organizational Change Management at SIRT3. Emili Bonilla Ardévol – Partner at Propelling Tech4. Emma Urbina – CEO and Co-founder at Jotavirtual5. Isabel Comas Segarra – Coordinator of Career Services and Guidance Programs at Barcelona ActivaWith the participation of companies, discover which digital profiles are most in demand, which skills are key, and which trends dominate the sector. The session includes guided networking, allowing you to make direct contacts with companies. An experience to guide you, help you learn, and boost your digital career.

title: A holistic view of artificial intelligence
description: 1. Antonio Serrano Acitores – Universidad Rey Juan Carlos – Coordinador de Digitalización, Innovación y Comunicación2. Elena González-Blanco García – Microsoft – Head of AI for Digital Natives in EMEA3. Cristina Casado Cañeque – Fundación Formación y Futuro – Deputy Executive Director (Moderadora)Discover how artificial intelligence is reshaping our world through a comprehensive dialogue that covers ethical, technical, and business perspectives.

title: DonesTIC Community: inspiration, connections, and community in a single gathering
description: Tara Chklovski – Founder & CEO of Technovation

title: Managing full infrastructure with GitOps and Crossplane
description: Allianz Technology aims to unify its Platform Engineering model to reduce complexity and enhance developer experience globally. Addressing fragmented tooling and complex pipelines, the strategy proposes a hierarchical architecture using GitOps, Argo CD, and Crossplane. It focuses on a multi-tenant Internal Developer Platform with clear responsibilities, reusable APIs, centralized governance, and secure automation for faster, safer delivery.

title: The Final Frontier with the Digital Twin
description: The Final Frontier with the Digital Twin. Have We Reached the Limit of Current Technology?

title: The New Era of Social Robotics: Turning Vision into Real-World Impact
description: Robotics is no longer a promise of the future, it’s a real-world driver of change. In this talk, Group Saltó’s CTO shares how we design and deploy applied robotic solutions, presenting the biggest active project in social robotics for the elderly. Explore the challenges and emerging horizons shaping the next generation of robotics.

title: From Brew to Breakthrough: Accelerating Innovation with AI
description: For over 150 years, Damm has driven innovation, and today AI accelerates the entire process from concept to prototype. The session will show how AI transforms ideation, design, testing, and validation, with practical examples of tools that reduce time‑to‑market and blend tradition with cutting‑edge technology

title: Europe’s moment to rise and finish the digital single market
description: Technology is a critical tool in the toolbox of geopolitical actors. Some companies are global players in their own right. Tech should be understood through a lense of power. Power to enjoy self-determination and sovereignty, power to innovate and grow, and power to defend or attack. The challenges of European tech dependencies can be shaped into a spectacular opportunity.

title: Back to Your Future: get ready for the tech market
description: 1. Javier Martínez – Leader – Claire Joster Technology en Claire Joster2. José María Flores – Regional Leader – Madrid en Claire Joster3. Jordi Valls – Senior Consultant 360 en Claire Joster4.Jordi Llahí – Sales and Marketing Leader en Claire JosterWe all would have benefited from knowing certain tips and lessons when we were students. Although we can’t go back, we can turn that reflection into an opportunity for others. With our knowledge, we provide students with guidance and practical tools to help them make decisions with greater confidence as they build their future. An experience that will help you travel toward the professional future you desire.

title: Beyond AI: the skills that make you employable by 42 Barcelona
description: The future of work is already here. AI, digitalization, and cybersecurity are transforming the job market. In this interactive workshop, we’ll explore the most in-demand skills for 2025 and 2030 according to the World Economic Forum and put them to the test in real time. Is learning to code still relevant in the age of AI? Join the 42 Barcelona team and discover how to boost your talent and stand out in the tech revolution.

title: More than a Hack – Final Pitching
description: Barça Innovation Hub, Metrica Sports, and MWCapital have launched “More than a Hack”, a pioneering competition that brings together generative artificial intelligence and real sports data to transform football analytics and tactical decision-making.During this session, the five finalist teams will take the spotlight to present their innovative solutions in front of a distinguished jury of industry experts and general audience.

title: Quickstart eBPF: Boosting Metrics and Security in Kubernetes
description: Dive into the power of eBPF with this hands-on quickstart workshop, designed to equip you with essential skills for enhancing observability and protection in Kubernetes environments. You’ll learn how eBPF enables kernel-level metrics collection, allowing real-time insights into pod performance, network traffic, and resource usage. Explore eBPF’s security applications, including runtime threat detection, access control enforcement, and anomaly monitoring.The workshop exercise will be conducted using Ubuntu 24.04. Students may use a local VM or a cloud instance as desired. If using a cloud instance, please test that you can access your cloud instance via PuTTY (Windows) or a terminal (Linux/Mac) before the session.

title: Why Players Don’t Quit: designing engaging difficulty
description: Designing engaging difficulty. Many difficult games fail because they confuse difficulty with frustration. In this talk, we will explore how Hell of an Office builds tension, precision, and repetition without breaking player motivation.

title: Model Context Protocol: the standard bridge between generative AI and the world
description: Aware of how data quality, function calling and agents have expanded the potential of generative AI, Anthropic released the Model Context Protocol (MCP), a model-agnostic open source framework that standardizes context exchange and actions between generative AI models and any software, such as cloud providers, databases, applications, filesystems, SaaS, frameworks… What does MCP enable today? What does it mean for the business? What does it mean for architects and developers? What are the security best practices? Let’s dive deep into the potential it brings to the industry.

title: Managing Transformation: How AI Reshapes Health Organizations & Physicians
description: 1. Jordi Guitart – M47 AI COMPANY – VP of Artificial Intelligence Health & Pharma2. Khalid Bouziane – Senior Data Scientist – ASHO3. Cristina Morgan – Ferrer – Innovation and CX Lead4. Ruth Gumbau – Journalist at Barcelona Health Hub (Moderator)This roundtable explores how AI is transforming healthcare organizations, reshaping clinical workflows, and redefining the role of physicians. Experts will discuss practical adoption, cultural and organizational change, ethics, and how professionals can lead this transition to deliver more efficient, personalized, and patient-centered care.

title: AI Transfer: From Lab to the Global Market
description: 1. Jordi Berenguer – Universitat Politècnica de Catalunya – BarcelonaTech (UPC) – Vice-rector for Transfer, Innovation and Entrepreneurship2. Aina Giral Balletbò – People Business partner at Giesecke + Devrient (G+D)3. (moderadora) M. José Jiménez Jiménez – Vice-rector for Teaching and Students – Universitat Politècnica de Catalunya – BarcelonaTech (UPC) – Universitat Politècnica de Catalunya4. Karina Gibert i Oliveres – Universitat Politècnica de Catalunya – BarcelonaTech -Dean of the Official College of Computer Engineering of Catalonia and member of the Team Board of IDEAI-UPC (Intelligent Data Science and Artificial Intelligence Research Center)5. Carla Gomez Cano – Co-founder of Theker Robotics, with a Bachelor’s degree in Industrial Electronic Engineering (EEBE-UPC) at Theker RoboticsHow do we transform research into real-world solutions? This session explores the keys to AI knowledge transfer between academia and the industr and organitations in need of digital talent. We will analyze successful public-private partnerships, the role of leading labs, and how UPC-driven innovation contributes to technological sovereignty. A debate about the innovation cycle and turning algorithms into drivers of economic and social impact.

title: Connect to Elevate: Volunteering Tech Skills for Impact
description: 1. Anna Baella – CodeWomen+ ESC at Open Cultural Center2. Ly-Ly Janusz – Education & Community Manager at Open Cultural Center3. Marta Kjorven – Fundraising Officer at Open Cultural Center

title: The Evolution of the Developer’s Role in the Era of Agentic AI
description: 1. Álex González – Director, Product Software Engineering (moderator)2. Marc Roger – Associate Director, Product Software Engineering at Wolters Kluwer3. Miguel Lopez – CTO at IMA ContigoThe emergence of Agentic AI is transforming software development and expanding the impact of the developer’s role. In this conversation between engineers, we will analyze how workflows are changing, which skills are becoming essential, and how to prepare for a reality in which humans and agents work together. A must-attend event for those who want to anticipate the future of development.

title: Fireside Chat: Kate Darling
From Data Stack Challenges to AI-Ready Scale: Lessons Learned with ClickHouse
description: 1. Gonçalo Pina (moderator) – Account Executive at Clickhouse2. Miguel Garcia – VP Eng at Factorial3. Pavel Pantiukhov – Engineering Team Lead at WallarmWe’ll dive into measurable outcomes achieved across both technical performance and business impact, and close with a forward-looking discussion on current AI and agent-driven initiatives. What’s top of mind as teams prepare their data infrastructure for the next wave of intelligent applications? Join us for practical insights, candid lessons learned, and real-world results.

title: How to Overcome Big Challenges
description: In this talk, Sara García Alonso shares how curiosity, courage, and perseverance can turn great challenges into opportunities for growth. Drawing from her experience as an oncology researcher and astronaut-in-training, she encourages reflection on the importance of taking risks, learning along the way, and never giving up. A presentation on personal leadership, resilience, and pushing beyond limits.

title: From Code to Capability: Unlocking New App Experiences with APIs
description: Developers already know how to build great apps but what if your apps could verify users instantly, prevent fraud by default, and adapt to real-world network conditions without added complexity? This session introduces Open Gateway and how standardised APIs unlock authentication, security, and network intelligence. Discover what’s newly possible, why it matters, and how it applies across fintech, gaming, AI, and mobility.

title: Robot Autonomy In An Unstructured World
description: Field Foundation Models (FFMs) are opening new possibilities for real-world autonomy. FFMs are uniquely designed to build context and experience to anticipate probable outcomes. Rather than requiring vast amounts of data to prepare for every scenario the robot could encounter, FieldAI FFM embraces the uncertainty of unknown environments to make safer and faster decisions with much greater efficiency without maps, GPS or pre-programmed paths.

title: AI at the Edge: Meetup with Qualcomm, Edge Impulse & Arduino
description: 1. Louis Moreau – Head of Developer Relations at Edge Impulse2. Jim Bruges – Senior Developer Relations Engineer at Edge Impulse

title: Hack Your CV: Key Tips to Optimize Your CV and Beat AI Filters
description: 1. Carlos Moratal – Regional Leader Claire Joster – Cataluña y Aragón at Claire Joster2. Maitane Urizarbarrena – People Partner at Eurofirms3. Katia Molero – Senior Consultant 360 at Claire JosterIn today’s recruitment processes, many résumés are automatically filtered before reaching human hands. Understanding how these systems work is key to improving job opportunities. This workshop provides practical tools and strategies to adapt your CV to AI filters, aiming especially to empower unemployed individuals, people with diverse profiles, or those in vulnerable situations.

title: How AI will change the future of chess
description: For decades, chess players have been terrified about the rise of computers and AI in the chess world. Many have believed that it will end the sport, because if computers are better than humans anyways, what is the point of having humans compete against eachother? This problem was famously raised in 1997 when the reigning world champion Garry Kasaprov lost to deepblue in a chess match. Today, that might seem like nothing unusual, but back then it symbolized that machines could excel at complex strategic tasks once thought to be uniquely human.Although many still fear what the future of a sport characterized by human intelligence holds in a world of computers, it is crucial to understand how computers and AI are going to, and already have, revolutionized chess. In many ways, the understanding of this centuries old game, is reaching levels we never thought it could reach. Levels, that would not be possible to reach, without computers. To talk more about this, we have Joost VandeVondole here, who can explain further, how Stockfish completely revolutionized the chess world.

title: Boosting productivity with AI: A hands-on workshop
description: 1. Antonio Serrano Acitores – Universidad Rey Juan Carlos – Coordinator of Digitalization, Innovation, and Communication2. Xavier Jofre Mestre – Fundación Formación y Futuro – Strategy Consultant3. Matilde Martínez Casanovas – IGEMA Business School – Managing DirectorBoost your productivity with a practical, hands-on tour of generative AI. Learn to use ChatGPT, Gemini and NotebookLM to research, draft, summarize and turn ideas into polished deliverables in minutes. Explore image and video models, plus coding assistants to automate repetitive tasks and generate assets on demand. You’ll also build cross-tool workflows that connect multiple AIs for faster, smarter delivery—live, measurable and in real time.

title: Hack Your Brain: Designing Bias-Free Technology
description: Designing quality technology goes far beyond code. This is the core idea of the FemFrame project. In this workshop, you’ll discover why a gender perspective is not an extra, but a key tool to prevent biases, errors, and digital products that don’t work. Through practical exercises and real cases, we’ll explore how the brain makes decisions, how biases are generated, and how they directly impact algorithms, data, software, and digital design. A workshop to hack your way of thinking and create better, fairer, and more efficient technology.

title: The Quantum Threat: Transitioning to Post-Quantum Security
description: 1. Vanesa Daza – Associate Professor – Vicerrector for Knowledge Transfer at Universitat Pompeu Fabra2. Erik Altelarrea Ferre – Suport a la Recerca (WICOM) at UPF3. Jordi Ribes Gonzalez – Suport a la Recerca (WICOM) at UPFQuantum computing is becoming a structural threat to today’s cryptographic backbone. Shor’s algorithm breaks the public-key schemes that secure the web, communications, and cloud. Even before large-scale quantum machines arrive, “harvest now, decrypt later” makes the risk immediate. In this session, we connect the dots from cryptographic fundamentals to the real impact of quantum progress, then walk through the practical shift to post-quantum cryptography: NIST-standardized algorithms, hybrid migration patterns, and what integration looks like in telecom and cloud environments. We also introduce fully homomorphic encryption (FHE), enabling computation directly on encrypted data and closing the long-standing “data-in-use” security gap. We’ll wrap up with a live technical demonstration of encrypted data processing using FHE.

title: Authentication for the AI Era: Build Passwordless Security in 5 Minutes
description: Build stronger authentication without adding friction. Discover a faster, more secure way to verify users beyond SMS codes, using mobile network capabilities to confirm device ownership and reduce fraud risks like SIM swap. Learn how to deliver a seamless, production-ready phone verification flow that works with your existing systems improving security, boosting conversion, and creating a smoother user experience in seconds.

title: Build Better Apps with Scalable Network Capabilities: Accelerating API Adoption
description: SMS authentication is slow, fragile, and increasingly vulnerable to fraud. In this session, Meta reveals how developers can move beyond one-time codes with API-driven Silent Authentication verifying users securely in the background with no codes required. Learn how it works at the network level, when to replace SMS, and how to integrate a faster, more secure, production-ready login flow that improves security and boosts conversion.

title: Certified Training to Boost Talent: Partnership Between IT Academy and UPC
description: 1. Martí Rodríguez (moderator) – Head of Technological Talent and Innovation at IT Academy-Barcelona Activa2. Gerard Campanera – Executive Director, Polytechnic Foundation of Catalonia at UPC3. Sara Díaz Roig – Executive Director of Strategic Sectors and Talent at Barcelona Activa4. Ana Ferrández – Data Analytics Alumni at Barcelona Activa_IT AcademyDiscover how the collaboration between Barcelona Activa and the Polytechnic University of Catalonia (UPC) promotes a digital training offer with university certification. This session features institutional perspectives and student experiences to understand the value of microcredentials in talent development and connecting with the job market.

title: Between Diapers and Deploys: Mothers Compiling the Future of Tech
description: 1. Olga Noguera – Empower Tech Spain – Co-Founder & Vice President (Moderador)2. Francis Santiago – Empower Tech Spain – Founder & CEO3. Kelly Cuesta – Latinas in Tech Spain – Founder & DirectorA panel discussion exploring how mothers navigate caregiving and a fast-moving cloud career. We’ll discuss the impact of maternity on professional growth, impostor syndrome, work–life balance challenges, and the need for visible female role models. Our goal: show that motherhood and tech can coexist and inspire a more inclusive future.

title: CANDELA: The Magic of Creating
description: What if magic wasn’t fantasy, but technology? We’ve always dreamed of transforming the world with simple gestures. In this presentation, you’ll discover how that ancient desire becomes reality. CANDELA translates gestures into light, sound, and image in real time, removing technical barriers and returning creative control to people. A new way to tell stories and create without limits. by Maria Badji, supported by Cátedra UAB-Cruïlla.

title: How AI is Redefining the Software Lifecycle
description: 1. Jordi Pujol Duarri – HEAD OF IA (moderador) at Vueling2. Dayana Avila – Quality Assurance at Vueling3. Victor Manuel Santiago Chaves – Software Engineering Lead at Vueling4. Xavier Armenteras Bosom – Data Scientist at VuelingGenerative artificial intelligence is transforming software development, from copilots that assist teams to agents capable of executing complex tasks. In this context, AI Engineering emerges, integrating software, data, QA, and MLOps to build reliable and scalable systems. In this roundtable, we will explore real-world experiences and challenges to understand how AI is redefining productivity across the software lifecycle.

title: Level up your apps: From Agent Protocols to Real-World API Innovation
description: Join this developer-focused session to explore how Network APIs are evolving toward autonomous, AI-driven connectivity. Discover how Agent Protocols and network-aware AI dynamically optimise performance, enable real-time decision-making, and unlock on-demand monetisation. Through live demos and real-world use cases, gain practical implementation insights you can apply immediately to build scalable, high-performing SuperApps.

title: Chaos, Spectacle & Systems: Crafting the Sound of GodsTV
description: After introducing GODSTV, we will go through the design of its dynamic music system within a roguelite structure, exploring how adaptive techno-driven scoring responds to systemic gameplay. We’ll also dive into the creative freedom the game’s bizarre universe enables for sound design, and how audio and music help frame the experience as a live TV spectacle rather than a traditional game session. Luis Quevedo, jourinalist and presenter (Moderator)

title: Open Gateway Hackathon: Live Finals
description: Experience the excitement of the Open Gateway Hackathon Live Finals, where the top teams showcase groundbreaking solutions built using advanced telecom network APIs powered by Nokia’s Network as Code platform. Watch live demos, hear rapid-fire pitches, and see innovation tested on real networks. It’s interactive and decided by you—cast your vote and help crown the next wave of telecom-powered innovation.

title: In-house algorithm development: experience and lessons learned
description: 1. Guillermo Prieto – Director of Information Technologies at Damm2. Daniel Roncel – Data Scientist at Nennisiwok

title: 87-minutes game. How AI is boosting the Barça’s edge
description: In an increasingly competitive sports industry, technology is no longer an accessory — it’s what amplifies what makes you unique. FC Barcelona showcase how AI is sharpening its competitive edge across performance, medical science, fan engagement, and commercial innovation. Leading clubs and institutions from around the world confirmed the same thesis: technology doesn’t replace identity, it amplifies it. For a 126-year-old club with 144,000 member-owners, AI means building smarter from within and buying better from outside. That’s Tech Your Edge.

title: CTO Leadership Lab: Communicate, Attract, and Retain Tech Talent
description: 1. Sandra Alonso – Program Leader at Eurofirms2. Marisa Martínez – Diversity & Inclusion Consultant at Eurofirms3. Mireia López – Diversity & Inclusion Consultant at EurofirmsCTOs and technology leaders face the challenge of turning business vision into technical decisions and transforming complexity into execution. Beyond technical expertise, their main challenge is to communicate clearly and effectively with diverse audiences, adapting the message, approach, and communication impact to connect technology, people, and business. The ultimate goal is to turn technological decisions into business actions.

title: How to train your robot
description: The Generative AI revolution has also reached robotics. World and VLC (Vision, Language, Action) models applied to new robotic systems equipped with powerful computing systems allow non-deterministic tasks to be solved. It is time to train robots. This workshop will discuss the latest developments in embedded AI and how the robotics industry is responding.

title: Adversarial Development: Treat Your AI Assistant as Untrusted
description: AI coding assistants write code at unprecedented speed—but speed without verification becomes technical debt. This talk introduces Adversarial Development, a quality framework inspired by GANs, SRE, and defense-in-depth. Treat AI as an adversarial generator and yourself as the discriminator using layered verification: prompt constraints, linting, runtime and visual checks. A practical playbook to ship faster without regressions or broken features

title: Leading in a World That Doesn’t Wait
description: Over 45 minutes, Pablo González Ruiz de la Torre and José Carlos Ruiz will engage in a calm and reflective conversation on humanistic leadership, addressing the intellectual, existential, and social dimensions of teams, and emphasizing the need to nurture identity and legacy as anchors in times of uncertainty.

title: AI with Human Values: The Challenge of Ethical and Cultural Alignment
description: 1. Paul Van Branteghem (moderador) – AI Advisor at SEDIA2. María Grandury – ML research engineer at SomosNLP3. Javier Aula-Blasco – Head of Trustworthiness & Evaluation, AI Institute at BSC-CNS4. Elena González-Blanco García – Head of AI for Digital Natives at MicrosoftWe will explore the process of aligning language models to follow our ethical and cultural values. We’ll examine how technology is trained to avoid biases and ensure that machines communicate in a way that reflects our values. The session will address the critical balance between system capability and applied safety filters to deliver accurate and safe responses while mitigating ethical risks.

title: How Public Institutions Shape Real Opportunities
description: 1. Gaizka Cañellas Ayerdi – Ironhack – Head of Business Development – B2G Director (Moderador)2. Alex Lobaco Poyatos – Consorci per a la Formació Contínua de Catalunya – Subdirector de Programes en Consorci per a la Formació Contínua de Catalunya3. Fernando Garrido Ferradanes – EOI – Director de B2G4. Miquel Carrion Molina – Servei Públic d’ocupació de Catalunya – Subdirector d’Ocupació Juvenil i Qualificació Professional en Servei Públic d’ocupació de CatalunyaWhat role do public institutions play in creating real opportunities for young talent and companies? In this roundtable, representatives from the Servei Públic d’Ocupació de Catalunya (SOC), the Consorci per a la Formació Contínua de Catalunya and Escuela de Organización Industrial (EOI) will discuss how public programmes and funding connect skills with real market needs, and how public actors can support talent development and business growth.

title: Secure Your MCP Servers with OAuth, JWT, SPIFFE and More
description: You’ve built an MCP server, but how do you secure it without rewriting everything? In this demo-led session, discover a smarter way to add authentication and authorisation without modifying or redeploying your servers. See how an MCP gateway can apply OAuth, JWT, and other identity systems at scale, and watch an AI agent securely interact with MCP tools showing how to simplify security across multiple servers.

title: Stop prompting, start building real solutions with AI
description: Move beyond simple prompting to unlock AI’s true potential by designing automated systems. This workshop guides you from manual chatting to building robust solutions, covering: – AI workflows: automate repetitive tasks, turning hours of work into seconds – Agentic AI: watch AI agents go beyond simple responses to take real-world action – Vibe coding: create functional tools using plain English descriptions, regardless of your coding experience

title: Prototype to Product: Scaling Edge AI Across use cases
description: Learn how to scale Edge AI applications across Qualcomm® developer kits and evaluation kits (EVKs) in this hands‑on workshop. Using keyword spotting as a practical example, you’ll see how the same ML pipeline can move from prototyping to deployment across multiple system‑on‑chips (SoCs). Explore how to select the right hardware, deploy models consistently, and build voice‑enabled experiences that run locally at the edge.

title: Building AI Agents with Spring AI
description: Building agents that actually work, ones that plan, reason, use tools, ask clarifying questions, and collaborate with other agents, requires more than just calling an LLM in a loop. It requires understanding agentic patterns and the protocols that connect them.In this workshop, we’ll build progressively more capable AI agents using Spring AI. We’ll start from the fundamentals, interacting with LLMs, crafting prompts, and working with structured output. From there, we’ll explore Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) for grounding responses in your own data, and tool calling for giving models the ability to take action in the real world. With these foundations in place, we’ll move into agentic territory, looking at workflow patterns and then building autonomous agents with recursive advisors and composable skills. Finally, we’ll cover the emerging agent protocol landscape: MCP for connecting agents to external tools and services, and A2A for enabling agents to discover and collaborate with each other. You’ll leave with working code and a clear understanding of how to build production-ready agents with Spring AI.

title: Network Abstraction: Powering Programmable Networks for the Agentic AI Era
description: 1. Sridhar Gollapudi – Global Telco Market Lead at Google2. Malcolm Deck – Software Engineering Manager at Google3. Micah Baker – Senior Product Manager at Google4. Alexandru Ciinaru – Product Manager Network APIs at Orange5. Chetan Narang – Head of Strategy and Innovation at ColtExplore how intent-driven, natural language deployment models are simplifying access to programmable networks. This session showcases real progress toward agentic AI frameworks, starting with a live demo of Google Firebase Phone Number Verification in action. See how Network APIs streamline identity and onboarding, then discover how Network Quality APIs power an automotive use case, enabling smarter, performance-aware applications.

title: The new era of talent access: Smarter searches, faster answers
description: Javier Carrión – Global Strategic Account Executive at Malt

title: Designing in the Dark: challenges of building a game without vision
description: What happens when you remove one of the most important elements in games: vision? In this session, Aleix and Xavi from Wheel of Games will explore the creative and technical challenges of developing a game designed to be played in darkness. From game design decisions to programming constraints, they will share the real difficulties of building mechanics, feedback systems, and player guidance without relying on visual clarity.

title: From Zero to Network-Aware AI in 15 Minutes: Building with CAMARA MCP
description: In this live, hands-on session, discover how to connect AI tools like Claude, ChatGPT, and Cursor to CAMARA network APIs and start building real applications in minutes. Watch as network capabilities are explored and turned into working code using AI-assisted development. This practical demo goes beyond theory showing you how to quickly create secure, network-aware apps using the tools you already use and trust.

title: Open Data Ecosystem in Spain: From Theory to Practice with Datos.gob
description: Open data has become an essential tool to drive transparency, innovation, and the development of new digital services. In this workshop, we will explore how open data has evolved, the relevant standards and technical regulations, as well as the main challenges for its adoption and future use. The session will conclude with a hands-on AI workshop applied to Datos.gob, focusing on the use of the Model Context Protocol (MCP).SPONSORED BY

title: The New Travel Project: A value chain driving a new travel experience
description: 1. Ana Perelló (Moderator) – Director of People HR at World2Meet2. Enric Riera – Chief Customer Officer at World2Meet3. Stefano Ceccarello – Chief Digital & Experience Officer at World2Meet4. Joan Barceló – CIO at W2MThe New Travel Project drives innovation to improve travel in all its aspects and for all its stakeholders. In the Talent Arena roundtable, World2Meet experts will showcase how this cross-cutting vision impacts distribution, technology, marketing, and digital experience, highlighting the importance of talent and collaboration to design the future of the tourism sector.

title: Talent isn’t retained, it’s cared for: context, community, and the future of work
description: For years, we have tried to solve the so-called “talent shortage” with processes, control, and quick fixes. This talk offers a new perspective. Drawing on their experience leading Spain’s leading agile community and creating shared learning spaces, Iratxe Kaltzakorta and María Morales explore why the real challenge isn’t attracting or retaining talent, but creating environments where people want to stay, grow, and thrive.

title: Hack Your Interview: Face Interviews with Confidence and Authenticity
description: 1. Sandra Alonso – Program Leader at Eurofirms2. Marisa Martínez – Diversity & Inclusion Consultant at Eurofirms3. Mireia López – Diversity & Inclusion Consultant at EurofirmsIn a tech sector where cognitive diversity drives innovation, approaching interviews with authenticity is key. Neurodiversity adds value to the recruitment process. This workshop provides practical tools for managing interviews and communication with confidence, helping neurodivergent and diverse individuals showcase their talent, strengthen self-awareness, and enter the tech sector with greater assurance.

title: Conversational Capital: The Hidden Asset Powering Agentic AI Experiences
description: As AI becomes agentic, conversations stop being interfaces and start becoming decision-making environments. In this talk, Hans van Dam introduces Conversational Capital: the trust, goodwill, and willingness to engage that accumulates—or collapses—through every interaction a brand has with people. Drawing on over a decade of field experience training 15,000+ professionals and working with more than 200 enterprise brands, Hans shows why most AI programs stall despite impressive technology—and how Agentic Experience Design (AXD) unlocks Conversational Capital by balancing agency and empathy at scale. This session offers a practical lens for leaders building AI systems that don’t just automate faster, but compound trust over time.

title: Design a Career You Love in Times of Ultra-Competitiveness
description: At a time when artificial intelligence is completely redefining the labor market, this talk explores how to design an authentic, sustainable, and competitive career within an increasingly demanding technological environment.We will explore practical frameworks for making professional decisions with judgment, vision, and a keen sense for opportunities. The session is structured around three pillars: identifying your own talents as the foundation for a real competitive advantage, pursuing curiosity as a driver of continuous professional reinvention, and strengthening execution skills in contexts of maximum uncertainty.The talk combines personal storytelling with data and studies that provide context and rigor. Attendees will leave with a practical framework and exercises to make better career decisions and navigate the future of work with greater clarity.This session explores how to rethink your professional career during this critical window of opportunity—not only to survive in an ultra-competitive labor market but to truly enjoy your career in the years ahead.

title: Confessions of a Vibecoding Engineer
description: AI is changing how we build software. What took weeks now happens in hours. Teams are discovering a new way of working that feels more like jamming than sprinting: “vibecoding”. Over the past year, I built dozens of AI-powered projects, some viral, some failures, all full of lessons. This talk shares stories, how AI reshapes engineering roles and workflows, and how to experiment and build at the speed of imagination.

title: HR Exchange: Inspirational Keynote
How Nestlé drives innovation through XR across the value chain
description: 1. Elizabeth Parcerisa – Adoption & Change Specialist at Nestlé2. Ares Subirà – Product Manager Immersive Experiences at Nestlé3. David Hinojosa – Tech Lead XR at Nestlé4. Clément Etienne – R&D Specialist Electronics en NestléIn this roundtable, we will explore how Nestlé leverages immersive technologies—such as augmented, virtual, and mixed reality—to create impact across multiple areas of the business. description: 1. Daniel González – Worldline – Test Manager2. Jose Maria Lopez Granados – Worldline – Head of Business Development & Innovation3. Santi Ristol Jorba – Worldline – Head of Digital Competence Centre4. We will share our strategy, technology stack and agile ways of working, including how we leverage Generative AI to deliver tangible Proofs of Concepts. We will present real results and our “from PoC to Product” scaling process, concluding with key lessons learned and practical recommendations you can apply in your own organization.

title: The future of the Edge AI and Physical AI
description: 1. Marc Pous Marín (moderator) – Developer Relations at Edge Impulse2. Leonardo Cavagnis – Snr Firmware Engineer & Developer Advocate at Arduino3. Louis Moreau – Head of Developer Relations at Edge Impulse4. Rajan Mistry – Evangelist Lead at Qualcomm5. Julian Caro Linares – Developer Relations at ArduinoExplore how Edge AI and Physical AI are transforming robotics and manufacturing—from low-power inference on microcontrollers to multimodal VLMs enabling real-world interaction. Discover energy-efficient hardware, privacy-first AI, and scalable ML deployments. What breakthroughs will redefine Physical AI in the coming years? Do not miss this conversation shaping tomorrow’s physical AI landscape with Arduino, Edge Impulse and Qualcomm.

title: Build your first neural network with PyTorch
description: In this workshop, we will first review the structure if neural networks, their history, as well as some important properties like the backpropagation algorithm. Then, we will learn how to use the python package PyTorch to implement a simple neural network and apply it to a toy dataset.

title: From fRusTrait-ion to Async Mastery: Become a Rust Trait Hero in One Hour
description: Ever felt the fRust-ration of Rust traits? This 60-minute session dives into the unsung heroes of Rust concurrency: Send and Sync. We’ll go beyond the surface and unpack what these traits really mean, how the compiler reasons about them, and what’s actually happening under the hood when your code crosses thread boundaries.

title: How to build an AI Agent for Energy Saving in 5G Networks using NVIDIA Blueprint
description: The Intent Driven Energy Saving Blueprint for 5G Networks demonstrates how Agentic AI can translate operator intent, expressed in natural language, into safe and explainable energy saving actions within a simulated 5G RAN environment. In this workshop, you will learn how to build a simple, explainable, and fully reproducible intent driven energy saving workflow for 5G networks using Agentic AI, synthetic KPI data, and a RAN simulator.

title: Leading without rescuing: mentoring for autonomous tech teams
description: Many leaders think they mentor, but actually tutor—directing, solving, and protecting. This creates dependency and limits growth. In fast-changing tech environments, teams need autonomy and accountability. This talk explores shifting to a mentoring mindset that challenges, empowers, and leads adults as adults—not as children with job titles.

title: Sewing AI: Innovation in Web Application Development with AI
description: Discover how Sewing AI is revolutionizing web application development at Inditex. This innovative platform leverages artificial intelligence to simplify and accelerate the creation of interactive prototypes, enabling teams to transform ideas into digital products quickly and efficiently. Sewing AI focuses on delivering visual results with the expertise of a UX specialist, while maintaining technical quality through a production-ready philosophy.

title: From Prototype to Product: Operationalizing Generative Personalization at Scale
description: Generative AI is reshaping personalization by making user experiences more expressive, intent-driven, and increasingly defined in natural language. Outputs become open-ended, contextual, and fine-grained, which disrupts much of the evaluation infrastructure that large-scale personalization has historically relied on. In these settings, “ground truth” is often underspecified, and quality depends on criteria like relevance, coherence, and user alignment rather than exact-match correctness. Yet human review and user studies do not scale. This talk explores LLM-as-a-Judge as a practical evaluation layer for generative and agentic systems. Drawing on recent research and public case studies, including published work from Spotify, we discuss how LLM-based judges can approximate human judgments, connect offline evaluation with human perception, and support meaningful assessment in low-feedback and cold-start settings. We then move from evaluation to optimization. When the judge signal is reliable, it can serve as an alignment signal for post-training and as a driver for automatic prompt and context optimization, enabling systematic comparisons, regression detection, and faster iteration cycles toward production-ready behavior.

title: Back to Your Future: get ready for the tech market
description: 1. Javier Martínez – Leader – Claire Joster Technology en Claire Joster2. José María Flores – Regional Leader – Madrid en Claire Joster3. Jordi Valls – Senior Consultant 360 en Claire Joster4.Jordi Llahí – Sales and Marketing Leader en Claire JosterWe all would have benefited from knowing certain tips and lessons when we were students. Although we can’t go back, we can turn that reflection into an opportunity for others. With our knowledge, we provide students with guidance and practical tools to help them make decisions with greater confidence as they build their future. An experience that will help you travel toward the professional future you desire.

title: Challenge Next Arena project presentations
description: The objective of the challenge is to rethink Talent Arena for 2027. Imagine and design how this event should evolve in order to remain relevant, inspiring, and transformative. As part of the NextArena Challenge, several student teams will present the projects they have developed to define how Talent Arena can evolve to become the leading global forum for digital talent. Proposals should address different strategic areas, combining creativity and innovation with analysis and a forward-looking vision, while taking into account the objectives outlined above. Participants are expected to design an experience that connects with new generations, delivers value to senior professionals, drives innovation, and leaves a lasting impact on the European digital ecosystem. In this context, the challenge aims to open its strategy to innovative ideas and differentiated proposals for the 2027 edition, using the previously described elements as a reference and aligning with the strategic and future-oriented vision that is being defined.

title: Beyond the Code: Skills That Drive Innovation
description: 1. Ainoa Irurre, Europe Talent Attraction Vice President at Schneider Electric2. Laura Sancho, Director IT Iberia & Project Management Officer at Schneider Electric3. Yolanda Triviño, CEO Institute for Futures Futures at Institute for Futures Futures4. Joana Barbany, Technology Business Development Director at Michael PageTechnology keeps evolving at full speed, but the skills that truly shape the future aren’t just technical. They’re human. In this session, experts from across the industry will share their experiences navigating a tech landscape where curiosity and adaptability matter just as much as technical expertise. Join this conversation to discover the human skills that can expand your opportunities, and set you apart from other professionals.

title: Turning Silos into Synergy Through InnerSource
description: InnerSource applies open source development principles inside an organization, enabling teams to collaborate across silos, share reusable components, and accelerate innovation. In this session, we’ll explore what InnerSource is, why it matters, and how it transforms the way companies build software. You’ll learn practical steps to start an InnerSource initiative, overcome common challenges, and foster a culture of transparency and shared ownership.

title: NEXT HR – Talent Magnetism: Employer Branding & Talent Acquisition
description: 1. Nuria Sabater – Employer Branding Manager at Perk2. Alinne Shedid – Global Talent Acquisition Manager at FairPlay Sports Media3. Tiago Santos (moderator) – VP of Community and Growth at Sesame HRAttracting digital talent is no longer just about offering competitive salaries or posting job openings. This panel explores how organizations can become talent magnets through an authentic Employee Value Proposition (EVP) aligned with their culture and values. Using real-world examples, it will cover what tech professionals are looking for today and how to stand out through purpose, projects, leadership, and employer branding built from within.

title: From “promstitution” to impact. How developers stay essential in the age of AI
description: In a world where anyone can automate software generation with AI, code no longer makes the difference. When intelligent agents are within everyone’s reach, the real value lies in knowing what to do with them. In this session, we will analyze why delegating everything to AI is not the solution, discuss human agency, and share tips on what makes developers stand out so they can remain relevant and deliver greater value where AI cannot.

title: Are agents the evolution of microservices?
description: Microservices changed how we build software, but AI agents are changing what software can do. In this talk, we will explore how agents can become the new cognitive microservices, capable of reasoning and acting within distributed systems. You’ll see demos with AWS serverless services that will help you understand how this new intelligent layer is transforming modern architecture.

title: NEXT HR – Market Intelligence for HR: HR Market Radar
description: Market data has become a key factor in guiding digital talent management. This session will examine how the use of objective data and its interpretation enables informed decision-making in areas such as compensation, critical skills, talent location, and workforce planning, integrating a practical view of the current market while anticipating its future impact.

title: AI @ PAL Robots
description: This presentation explores how artificial intelligence is driving the development of humanoid robotics. We will examine the most widely used techniques and see how they are integrated into these systems, demonstrating how they enable robots to be more adaptive, safe, and collaborative. The session will also reflect on the potential of these technologies to improve daily life, assist with complex tasks, and expand human capabilities.

title: AI Models for Marketing & Growth Strategy​
description: In this hands-on session, we will demonstrate how to use AI models to design and execute real marketing and growth strategies, regardless of experience level. Through clear examples and applied use cases, participants will learn how to select models, structure prompts, and build workflows that help attract, convert, and optimize business decisions. Applied AI you can start using from day one.

You’ll build an end-to-end cascaded AI application in the edge, balancing power efficiency and accuracy on real hardware. Bring your laptop and your curiosity!

It will also examine the balance between technological efficiency, candidate experience, and human judgment, focusing on AI-augmented, people-centered decision-making.

title: From Adoption to Impact: How Adoption Accelerates AI & Cloud Transformation in Enterprise Teams
description: Enterprises invest heavily in AI and cloud, yet struggle to realize value due to adoption gaps, not technology. This talk shares practical frameworks from enterprise and SaaS programs to improve onboarding, role-based training, and success metrics that accelerate adoption, reduce time-to-value, and drive sustained business impact.

title: What Does It Take to Build a Real-Time Data Platform?
description: Modern AI systems—from recommendation engines to autonomous LLM agents—depend on fast, reliable access to fresh data. Feature retrieval, contextual enrichment, analytics, and observability all require sub-second responses, even at terabyte or petabyte scale, to deliver truly interactive experiences. In this session, we’ll explore how ClickHouse enables real-time analytics at scale and how it fits into end-to-end AI architectures.

description: 1. Javier Martínez – Leader – Claire Joster Technology at Claire Joster2. José María Flores – Regional Leader – Madrid at Claire Joster3. Jordi Valls – Senior Consultant 360 at Claire Joster4. Jordi Llahí – Sales and Marketing Leader at Claire JosterThe job interview is a demanding process that requires preparation and strategy. In this workshop, you will find the most realistic simulator to help you avoid mistakes in an actual job interview.

title: Scaling Science at large facilities: ALBA and BSC
description: The Barcelona Supercomputing Center (BSC) and the ALBA Synchrotron are two major research hubs located in Barcelona. ALBA, a particle accelerator for experimental science, produces huge volumes of ultra-high-resolution data. BSC, the Spain’s national supercomputing center, hosts the MareNostrum and multiple research groups. This talk will examine the essential role of computing in driving the frontier of scientific discovery.

title: NEXT HR – Rethinking Access to Digital Talent
description: Access to digital talent is evolving due to imbalances between profiles, increasing demand for specialization, and the prioritization of experienced profiles over the development of early-stage talent. This session will address new ways to identify and activate talent beyond traditional hiring, from early potential detection to flexible collaboration models, and their strategic implications for HR.

title: The Future of Enterprise AI Governance
description: AI is now democratized, reshaping every role and organization. As it becomes embedded in work, how do we keep it trustworthy, compliant, and safe? Enterprise AI Governance is critical, yet most organizations are just beginning. Oliver Patel, who leads AI governance at AstraZeneca and authored Fundamentals of AI Governance, shares how regulations are evolving, why AI literacy is essential, and how agentic AI changes the game.

title: 20,000 Blunders in Tech Leadership
description: 1. Àlex Rodriguez Bacardit – Founder & CEO at MarsBased (moderator)2. Ricard Clau – IA & DevOps at RigorAlliance3. Xavier Tous – CTO at XCEED4. Pau Minoves – Founder at Ahead5. Marc Oliveras Xiol – VP of Engineering at Emburse and CTO at CaptioThe odyssey of the technical leader begins with a brilliant engineer who, after being called to cross the “technical career chasm,” must abandon their identity as a “chef in the kitchen” focused on code in order to learn how to achieve results through the hands of others. In this “choose your own adventure” labyrinth, our hero faces monsters such as technical debt, which they must learn to translate into the financial language of spreadsheets in order to fight it strategically.Through the trials of the “You Build It, You Run It” philosophy and the demolition of organizational silos, they discover that their true strength lies in automation and shared product ownership. By invoking sacred tools such as “Priority Buckets” and OKRs to align their tribe with business objectives, and by applying the balm of blameless postmortems to transform mistakes into systemic learning, the leader returns transformed into the glue that binds technical talent with strategic vision.Ultimately, the journey reveals that their mission is not only to plant technological “baby trees” for the future, but also to safeguard their own mental health and that of their team at the lonely summit of leadership. In this presentation, Àlex Rodríguez Bacardit and colleagues will discuss the complex path to technological leadership and share some of their greatest successes and failures.

title: NEXT HR – Grow to Stay: Developing Digital Talent to Improve Retention
description: 1. Alfonso Bustos – Head of Digital Academy at RICOH EUROPE2. James Basha – CEO at Enterprelearners3. Jessica Ferreira – Digital Transformation Office Lead at SEAT4. Giulio Toscani (moderador) – Senior Lecturer at UPF Barcelona School of ManagementRetaining digital talent is one of the main challenges facing HR teams today. Drawing on real-world experiences, this session will explore how professional development, upskilling, and continuous learning serve as key levers to strengthen engagement and retention. The focus will be on practical approaches that connect skills development with talent motivations and organizational needs.

Using DORA + BaFin regulations as catalyst, OpenSource adoption can turn compliance into a competitive advantage. With a data-driven approach, implementing an Open Source Program Office (OSPO) drives immediate ROI (44-60% cost savings, 25-40% faster delivery).
